# vim: ft=python fileencoding=utf-8 sts=4 sw=4 et:\n2: \n3: # Copyright 2014-2019 Florian Bruhin (The Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>\n4: #\n5: # This file is part of qutebrowser.\n6: #\n7: # If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.\n19: \n20: \"\"\"Types for options in qutebrowser's configuration.\n21: \n22: Those types are used in configdata.yml as type of a setting.\n23: \n24: Most of them are pretty generic, but some of them are e.g. specific String\n25: subclasses with valid_values set, as that particular \"type\" is used multiple\n26: times in the config.\n27: \n28: A setting value can be represented in three different ways:\n29: \n30: 1) As an object which can be represented in YAML:\n31:    str, list, dict, int, float, True/False/None\n32:    This is what qutebrowser actually saves internally, and also what it gets\n33:    from the YAML or config.py.\n34: 2) As a string. This is e.g. used by the :set command.\n35: 3) As the value the code which uses it expects, e.g. enum members.\n36: \n37: Config types can do different conversations:\n38: \n39: - Object to string with .to_str() (1 -> 2)\n40: - String to object with .from_str() (2 -> 1)\n41: - Object to code with .to_py() (1 -> 3)\n42:   This also validates whether the object is actually correct (type/value).\n43: \"\"\"\n44: \n45: import re\n46: import html\n47: import codecs\n48: import os.path\n49: import itertools\n50: import warnings\n51: import datetime\n52: import functools\n53: import operator\n54: import json\n55: import typing\n56: \n57: import attr\n58: import yaml\n59: from PyQt5.QtCore import QUrl, Qt\n60: from PyQt5.QtGui import QColor, QFont\n61: from PyQt5.QtWidgets import QTabWidget, QTabBar\n62: from PyQt5.QtNetwork import QNetworkProxy\n63: \n64: from qutebrowser.misc import objects\n65: from qutebrowser.config import configexc, configutils\n66: from qutebrowser.utils import (standarddir, utils, qtutils, urlutils, urlmatch,\n67:                                usertypes)\n68: from qutebrowser.keyinput import keyutils\n69: \n70: \n71: class _SystemProxy:\n72: \n73:     pass\n74: \n75: \n76: SYSTEM_PROXY = _SystemProxy()  # Return value for Proxy type\n77: \n78: # Taken from configparser\n79: BOOLEAN_STATES = {'1': True, 'yes': True, 'true': True, 'on': True,\n80:                   '0': False, 'no': False, 'false': False, 'off': False}\n81: \n82: \n83: _Completions = typing.Optional[typing.Iterable[typing.Tuple[str, str]]]\n84: _StrUnset = typing.Union[str, configutils.Unset]\n85: _StrUnsetNone = typing.Union[None, str, configutils.Unset]\n86: \n87: \n88: class ValidValues:\n89: \n90:     \"\"\"Container for valid values for a given type.\n91: \n92:     Attributes:\n93:         values: A list with the allowed untransformed values.\n94:         descriptions: A dict with value/desc mappings.\n95:         generate_docs: Whether to show the values in the docs.\n96:     \"\"\"\n97: \n98:     def __init__(self,\n99:                  *values: typing.Union[str,\n100:                                        typing.Dict[str, str],\n101:                                        typing.Tuple[str, str]],\n102:                  generate_docs: bool = True) -> None:\n103:         if not values:\n104:             raise ValueError(\"ValidValues with no values makes no sense!\")\n105:         self.descriptions = {}  # type: typing.Dict[str, str]\n106:         self.values = []  # type: typing.List[str]\n107:         self.generate_docs = generate_docs\n108:         for value in values:\n109:             if isinstance(value, str):\n110:                 # Value without description\n111:                 self.values.append(value)\n112:             elif isinstance(value, dict):\n113:                 # List of dicts from configdata.yml\n114:                 assert len(value) == 1, value\n115:                 value, desc = list(value.items())[0]\n116:                 self.values.append(value)\n117:                 self.descriptions[value] = desc\n118:             else:\n119:                 # (value, description) tuple\n120:                 self.values.append(value[0])\n121:                 self.descriptions[value[0]] = value[1]\n122: \n123:     def __contains__(self, val: str) -> bool:\n124:         return val in self.values\n125: \n126:     def __iter__(self) -> typing.Iterator[str]:\n127:         return self.values.__iter__()\n128: \n129:     def __repr__(self) -> str:\n130:         return utils.get_repr(self, values=self.values,\n131:                               descriptions=self.descriptions)\n132: \n133:     def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:\n134:         assert isinstance(other, ValidValues)\n135:         return (self.values == other.values and\n136:                 self.descriptions == other.descriptions)\n137: \n138: \n139: class BaseType:\n140: \n141:     \"\"\"A type used for a setting value.\n142: \n143:     Attributes:\n144:         none_ok: Whether to allow None (or an empty string for :set) as value.\n145: \n146:     Class attributes:\n147:         valid_values: Possible values if they can be expressed as a fixed\n148:                       string. ValidValues instance.\n149:     \"\"\"\n150: \n151:     def __init__(self, none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n152:         self.none_ok = none_ok\n153:         self.valid_values = None  # type: typing.Optional[ValidValues]\n154: \n155:     def get_name(self) -> str:\n156:         \"\"\"Get a name for the type for documentation.\"\"\"\n157:         return self.__class__.__name__\n158: \n159:     def get_valid_values(self) -> typing.Optional[ValidValues]:\n160:         \"\"\"Get the type's valid values for documentation.\"\"\"\n161:         return self.valid_values\n162: \n163:     def _basic_py_validation(\n164:             self, value: typing.Any,\n165:             pytype: typing.Union[type, typing.Tuple[type, ...]]) -> None:\n166:         \"\"\"Do some basic validation for Python for validation and returns the\n242:         unaltered value. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01T3VRx4LUbVFEUqCne1CMit","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Ah! I see it now! Look at line 1143-1144:```python\nint_vals = [self._parse_value(kind, val)\n            for kind, val in zip(kind, vals)]\n```\n\nThis is a variable shadowing bug! The `kind` variable is being reused in the list comprehension, which shadows the outer `kind` variable. So:- `kind = 'hsv'` initially (the color space name)\n- In the loop: `for kind, val in zip(kind, vals)` where the `kind` variable is being reused\n\nSo on the second iteration of `zip(kind, vals)`:- `kind` is now being overwritten to be 's' (the second character), 'v' (the third character), etc.\n\nBut wait, if `kind` is initially `'hsv'`, then `zip(kind, vals)` should be:- `zip('hsv', ['10%', '10%', '10%'])` → `[('h', '10%'), ('s', '10%'), ('v', '10%')]`\n\nSo the loop iterates as:\n1. `kind='h'`, `val='10%'` → calls `_parse_value('h', '10%')` ✓\n2. `kind='s'`, `val='10%'` → calls `_parse_value('s', '10%')` ✓\n3. `kind='v'`, `val='10%'` → calls `_parse_value('v', '10%')` ✓\n\nWait, that should work correctly! The `kind` in the list comprehension is the second `kind` variable (the character from 'hsv'), not the outer `kind` (which is 'hsv').\n\nBut hold on... when we call `_parse_value(kind, val)` inside the list comprehension, which `kind` does it use? It should use the one from the `for` clause.\n\n\nActually, I'm overcomplicating this. Let me look at the actual code more carefully. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_017tGfnfgQetomd7KVXvoGn1","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_017Kj9gAXYGF6ravXVL6rpDe","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/regression_test.py","content":"\"\"\"Regression test for HSV color percentage parsing bug.\n\nThis test verifies that HSV colors with percentage values are parsed correctly,\nwith hue scaling to 0-359 degrees instead of 0-255.\n\nThe bug is that `hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)` is parsed as `(255, 255, 255)` instead\nof `(359, 255, 255)`. This affects both HSV and HSVA color parsing.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport sys\nimport os\n\n# Add the qutebrowser directory to the path so we can import modules\nsys.path.insert(0, '/app')\n\nimport pytest\nfrom PyQt5.QtGui import QColor\n\n# Import the color type handler\nfrom qutebrowser.config.configtypes import QtColor\n\n\nclass TestHSVPercentageScaling:\n    \"\"\"Test that HSV colors with percentages scale hue correctly.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_hsv_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 100% hue maps to 359 degrees, not 255.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 100% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # With the bug, hue would be 255 (100% of 255)\n        # With the fix, hue should be 359 (100% of 359)\n        assert h == 359, f\"Expected hue=359 for 100%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 255, f\"Expected saturation=255, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 255, f\"Expected value=255, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 50% hue maps to ~179 degrees, not ~127.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 50% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(50%, 50%, 50%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # With the bug, hue would be 127 (50% of 255)\n        # With the fix, hue should be 179 (50% of 359)\n        assert h == 179, f\"Expected hue=179 for 50%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 127 or s == 128, f\"Expected saturation~127, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 127 or v == 128, f\"Expected value~127, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_10_percent(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 10% scales correctly.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 10% values\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(10%, 10%, 10%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # 10% of 359 ≈ 35.9 → 35\n        # 10% of 255 ≈ 25.5 → 25\n        assert h == 35, f\"Expected hue=35 for 10%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 25 or s == 26, f\"Expected saturation~25, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 25 or v == 26, f\"Expected value~25, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsva_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that HSVA (with alpha) also scales hue correctly.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSVA color with 100% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%)')\n\n        # Get the HSVA components\n        h, s, v, a = result.getHsv()\n\n        # With the bug, hue would be 255\n        # With the fix, hue should be 359\n        assert h == 359, f\"Expected hue=359 for 100%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 255, f\"Expected saturation=255, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 255, f\"Expected value=255, got {v}\"\n        assert a == 255, f\"Expected alpha=255, got {a}\"\n\n    def test_hsva_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that HSVA with 50% hue scales correctly.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSVA color with 50% values\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsva(50%, 50%, 50%, 50%)')\n\n        # Get the HSVA components\n        h, s, v, a = result.getHsv()\n\n        # 50% of 359 ≈ 179.5 → 179\n        # 50% of 255 ≈ 127.5 → 127\n        assert h == 179, f\"Expected hue=179 for 50%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 127 or s == 128, f\"Expected saturation~127, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 127 or v == 128, f\"Expected value~127, got {v}\"\n        assert a == 127 or a == 128, f\"Expected alpha~127, got {a}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_25_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 25% hue maps to ~89-90 degrees, not ~63-64.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 25% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(25%, 25%, 25%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # 25% of 359 ≈ 89.75 → 89\n        # 25% of 255 (buggy) ≈ 63.75 → 63\n        assert h == 89, f\"Expected hue=89 for 25%, got {h}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_integer_values_unchanged(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that integer HSV values work as expected.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with integer values (0-359 for hue, 0-255 for s/v)\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(180, 128, 64)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # Integer values should be used directly\n        assert h == 180, f\"Expected hue=180, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 128, f\"Expected saturation=128, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 64, f\"Expected value=64, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsva_integer_values_unchanged(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that integer HSVA values work as expected.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSVA color with integer values\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsva(180, 128, 64, 200)')\n\n        # Get the HSVA components\n        h, s, v, a = result.getHsv()\n\n        # Integer values should be used directly\n        assert h == 180, f\"Expected hue=180, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 128, f\"Expected saturation=128, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 64, f\"Expected value=64, got {v}\"\n        assert a == 200, f\"Expected alpha=200, got {a}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_mixed_percentage_and_integer(self):\n        \"\"\"Test HSV with mixed percentage and integer values.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # This should fail with a validation error since all values must be\n        # either percentages or integers, not mixed\n        # But let's test with all percentages for correctness\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(25%, 64, 32)')\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # This affects both HSV and HSVA color parsing.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport sys\nimport os\n\n# Add the qutebrowser directory to the path so we can import modules\nsys.path.insert(0, '/app')\n\nimport pytest\nfrom PyQt5.QtGui import QColor\n\n# Import the color type handler\nfrom qutebrowser.config.configtypes import QtColor\n\n\nclass TestHSVPercentageScaling:\n    \"\"\"Test that HSV colors with percentages scale hue correctly.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_hsv_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 100% hue maps to 359 degrees, not 255.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 100% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # With the bug, hue would be 255 (100% of 255)\n        # With the fix, hue should be 359 (100% of 359)\n        assert h == 359, f\"Expected hue=359 for 100%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 255, f\"Expected saturation=255, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 255, f\"Expected value=255, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 50% hue maps to ~179 degrees, not ~127.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 50% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(50%, 50%, 50%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # With the bug, hue would be 127 (50% of 255)\n        # With the fix, hue should be 179 (50% of 359)\n        assert h == 179, f\"Expected hue=179 for 50%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 127 or s == 128, f\"Expected saturation~127, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 127 or v == 128, f\"Expected value~127, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_10_percent(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 10% scales correctly.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 10% values\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(10%, 10%, 10%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # 10% of 359 ≈ 35.9 → 35\n        # If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.\\n19: \\n20: \\\"\\\"\\\"Types for options in qutebrowser's configuration.\\n21: \\n22: Those types are used in configdata.yml as type of a setting.\\n23: \\n24: Most of them are pretty generic, but some of them are e.g. specific String\\n25: subclasses with valid_values set, as that particular \\\"type\\\" is used multiple\\n26: times in the config.\\n27: \\n28: A setting value can be represented in three different ways:\\n29: \\n30: 1) As an object which can be represented in YAML:\\n31:    str, list, dict, int, float, True/False/None\\n32:    This is what qutebrowser actually saves internally, and also what it gets\\n33:    from the YAML or config.py.\\n34: 2) As a string. This is e.g. used by the :set command.\\n35: 3) As the value the code which uses it expects, e.g. enum members.\\n36: \\n37: Config types can do different conversations:\\n38: \\n39: - Object to string with .to_str() (1 -> 2)\\n40: - String to object with .from_str() (2 -> 1)\\n41: - Object to code with .to_py() (1 -> 3)\\n42:   This also validates whether the object is actually correct (type/value).\\n43: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n44: \\n45: import re\\n46: import html\\n47: import codecs\\n48: import os.path\\n49: import itertools\\n50: import warnings\\n51: import datetime\\n52: import functools\\n53: import operator\\n54: import json\\n55: import typing\\n56: \\n57: import attr\\n58: import yaml\\n59: from PyQt5.QtCore import QUrl, Qt\\n60: from PyQt5.QtGui import QColor, QFont\\n61: from PyQt5.QtWidgets import QTabWidget, QTabBar\\n62: from PyQt5.QtNetwork import QNetworkProxy\\n63: \\n64: from qutebrowser.misc import objects\\n65: from qutebrowser.config import configexc, configutils\\n66: from qutebrowser.utils import (standarddir, utils, qtutils, urlutils, urlmatch,\\n67:                                usertypes)\\n68: from qutebrowser.keyinput import keyutils\\n69: \\n70: \\n71: class _SystemProxy:\\n72: \\n73:     pass\\n74: \\n75: \\n76: SYSTEM_PROXY = _SystemProxy()  # Return value for Proxy type\\n77: \\n78: # Taken from configparser\\n79: BOOLEAN_STATES = {'1': True, 'yes': True, 'true': True, 'on': True,\\n80:                   '0': False, 'no': False, 'false': False, 'off': False}\\n81: \\n82: \\n83: _Completions = typing.Optional[typing.Iterable[typing.Tuple[str, str]]]\\n84: _StrUnset = typing.Union[str, configutils.Unset]\\n85: _StrUnsetNone = typing.Union[None, str, configutils.Unset]\\n86: \\n87: \\n88: class ValidValues:\\n89: \\n90:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Container for valid values for a given type.\\n91: \\n92:     Attributes:\\n93:         values: A list with the allowed untransformed values.\\n94:         descriptions: A dict with value/desc mappings.\\n95:         generate_docs: Whether to show the values in the docs.\\n96:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n97: \\n98:     def __init__(self,\\n99:                  *values: typing.Union[str,\\n100:                                        typing.Dict[str, str],\\n101:                                        typing.Tuple[str, str]],\\n102:                  generate_docs: bool = True) -> None:\\n103:         if not values:\\n104:             raise ValueError(\\\"ValidValues with no values makes no sense!\\\")\\n105:         self.descriptions = {}  # ValidValues instance.\\n149:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n150: \\n151:     def __init__(self, none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\\n152:         self.none_ok = none_ok\\n153:         self.valid_values = None  #    return self.valid_values\\n162: \\n163:     def _basic_py_validation(\\n164:             self, value: typing.Any,\\n165:             pytype: typing.Union[type, typing.Tuple[type, ...]]) ->                         value: typing.Any) -> None:\\n213:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Make sure the given value doesn't contain surrogate escapes.\\n214: \\n215:         This is used for values passed to json.dump, as it can't handle those.\\n216:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n217:         if not isinstance(value, str):\\n218:             return\\n219:         if any(ord(c) > 0xFFFF for c in value):\\n220: If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.\\n19: \\n20: \\\"\\\"\\\"Types for options in qutebrowser's configuration.\\n21: \\n22: Those types are used in configdata.yml as type of a setting.\\n23: \\n24: Most of them are pretty generic, but some of them are e.g. specific String\\n25: subclasses with valid_values set, as that particular \\\"type\\\" is used multiple\\n26: times in the config.\\n27: \\n28: A setting value can be represented in three different ways:\\n29: \\n30: 1) As an object which can be represented in YAML:\\n31:    str, list, dict, int, float, True/False/None\\n32:    This is what qutebrowser actually saves internally, and also what it gets\\n33:    from the YAML or config.py.\\n34: 2) As a string. This is e.g. used by the :set command.\\n35: 3) As the value the code which uses it expects, e.g. enum members.\\n36: \\n37: Config types can do different conversations:\\n38: \\n39: - Object to string with .to_str() (1 -> 2)\\n40: - String to object with .from_str() (2 -> 1)\\n41: - Object to code with .to_py() (1 -> 3)\\n42:   This also validates whether the object is actually correct (type/value).\\n43: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n44: \\n45: import re\\n46: import html\\n47: import codecs\\n48: import os.path\\n49: import itertools\\n50: import warnings\\n51: import datetime\\n52: import functools\\n53: import operator\\n54: import json\\n55: import typing\\n56: \\n57: import attr\\n58: import yaml\\n59: from PyQt5.QtCore import QUrl, Qt\\n60: from PyQt5.QtGui import QColor, QFont\\n61: from PyQt5.QtWidgets import QTabWidget, QTabBar\\n62: from PyQt5.QtNetwork import QNetworkProxy\\n63: \\n64: from qutebrowser.misc import objects\\n65: from qutebrowser.config import configexc, configutils\\n66: from qutebrowser.utils import (standarddir, utils, qtutils, urlutils, urlmatch,\\n67:                                usertypes)\\n68: from qutebrowser.keyinput import keyutils\\n69: \\n70: \\n71: class _SystemProxy:\\n72: \\n73:     pass\\n74: \\n75: \\n76: SYSTEM_PROXY = _SystemProxy()  # Return value for Proxy type\\n77: \\n78: # Taken from configparser\\n79: BOOLEAN_STATES = {'1': True, 'yes': True, 'true': True, 'on': True,\\n80:                   '0': False, 'no': False, 'false': False, 'off': False}\\n81: \\n82: \\n83: _Completions = typing.Optional[typing.Iterable[typing.Tuple[str, str]]]\\n84: _StrUnset = typing.Union[str, configutils.Unset]\\n85: _StrUnsetNone = typing.Union[None, str, configutils.Unset]\\n86: \\n87: \\n88: class ValidValues:\\n89: \\n90:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Container for valid values for a given type.\\n91: \\n92:     Attributes:\\n93:         values: A list with the allowed untransformed values.\\n94:         descriptions: A dict with value/desc mappings.\\n95:         generate_docs: Whether to show the values in the docs.\\n96:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n97: \\n98:     def __init__(self,\\n99:                  *values: typing.Union[str,\\n100:                                        typing.Dict[str, str],\\n101:                                        typing.Tuple[str, str]],\\n102:                  generate_docs: bool = True) -> None:\\n103:         if not values:\\n104:             raise ValueError(\\\"ValidValues with no values makes no sense!\\\")\\n105:         self.descriptions = {}  # type: typing.Dict[str, str]\\n106:         self.values = []  # type: typing.List[str]\\n107:         self.generate_docs = generate_docs\\n108:         for value in values:\\n109:             if isinstance(value, str):\\n110:                 # Value without description\\n111:                 self.values.append(value)\\n112:             elif isinstance(value, dict):\\n113:                 # List of dicts from configdata.yml\\n114:                 assert len(value) == 1, value\\n115:                 value, desc = list(value.items())[0]\\n116:                 self.values.append(value)\\n117:                 self.descriptions[value] = desc\\n118:             else:\\n119:                 # (value, description) tuple\\n120:                 self.values.append(value[0])\\n121:                 self.descriptions[value[0]] = value[1]\\n122: \\n123:     def __contains__(self, val: str) -> bool:\\n124:         return val in self.values\\n125: \\n126:     def __iter__(self) -> typing.Iterator[str]:\\n127:         return self.values.__iter__()\\n128: \\n129:     def __repr__(self) -> str:\\n130:         return utils.get_repr(self, values=self.values,\\n131:                               descriptions=self.descriptions)\\n132: \\n133:     def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:\\n134:         assert isinstance(other, ValidValues)\\n135:         return (self.values == other.values and\\n136:                 self.descriptions == other.descriptions)\\n137: \\n138: \\n139: class BaseType:\\n140: \\n141:     \\\"\\\"\\\"A type used for a setting value.\\n142: \\n143:     Attributes:\\n144:         none_ok: Whether to allow None (or an empty string for :set) as value.\\n145: \\n146:     Class attributes:\\n147:         valid_values: Possible values if they can be expressed as a fixed\\n148:                       string. ValidValues instance.\\n149:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n150: \\n151:     def __init__(self, none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\\n152:         self.none_ok = none_ok\\n153:         self.valid_values = None  # type: typing.Optional[ValidValues]\\n154: \\n155:     def get_name(self) -> str:\\n156:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get a name for the type for documentation.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n157:         return self.__class__.__name__\\n158: \\n159:     def get_valid_values(self) -> typing.Optional[ValidValues]:\\n160:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get the type's valid values for documentation.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n161:         return self.valid_values\\n162: \\n163:     def _basic_py_validation(\\n164:             self, value: typing.Any,\\n165:             pytype: typing.Union[type, typing.Tuple[type, ...]]) -> None:\\n166:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Do some basic validation for Python values (emptyness, type).\\n167: \\n168:         Arguments:\\n169:             value: The value to check.\\n170:             pytype: A Python type to check the value against.\\n171:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n172:         if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\\n173:             return\\n174: \\n175:         if (value is None or (pytype == list and value == []) or\\n176:                 (pytype == dict and value == {})):\\n177:             if not self.none_ok:\\n178:                 raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \\\"may not be null!\\\")\\n179:             else:\\n180:                 return\\n181: \\n182:         if (not isinstance(value, pytype) or\\n183:                 pytype is int and isinstance(value, bool)):\\n184:             if isinstance(pytype, tuple):\\n185:                 expected = ' or '.join(typ.__name__ for typ in pytype)\\n186:             else:\\n187:                 expected = pytype.__name__\\n188:             raise configexc.ValidationError(\\n189:                 value, \\\"expected a value of type {} but got {}.\\\".format(\\n190:                     expected, type(value).__name__))\\n191: \\n192:         if isinstance(value, str):\\n193:             self._basic_str_validation(value)\\n194: \\n195:     def _basic_str_validation(self, value: str) -> None:\\n196:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Do some basic validation for string values.\\n197: \\n198:         This checks that the value isn't empty and doesn't contain any\\n199:         unprintable chars.\\n200: \\n201:         Arguments:\\n202:             value: The value to check.\\n203:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n204:         assert isinstance(value, str), value\\n205:         if not value and configexc.ValidationError(\\n209:                 value, \\\"may not contain unprintable chars!\\\")\\n210: \\n211:     def _validate_surrogate_escapes(self, full_value: typing.Any,\\n212:                                     value: typing.Any) -> None:\\n213:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Make sure the given value doesn't contain surrogate escapes.\\n214: \\n215:         This is used for values passed to json.dump, as it can't handle those.\\n216:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n217:         if not isinstance(value, str):\\n218:             return\\n219:         if any(ord(c) > 0xFFFF for c in value):\\n220:             raise configexc.ValidationError(\\n221:                 full_value, \\\"may not contain surrogate escapes!\\\")\\n222: \\n223:     def _validate_valid_values(self, value: str) -> None:\\n224:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Validate value against possible values.\\n225: \\n226:         The default implementation checks the value against self.valid_values\\n227:         if it was defined.\\n228: \\n229:         Args:\\n230:             value: The value to validate.\\n231:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n232:         if self.valid_values is not None:\\n233:             if value not in '.join(self.valid_values)))\\n237: \\n238:     def from_str(self, value: str) -> typing.Any:\\n239:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get the setting value from a string.\\n240: \\n241:         By default this invokes to_py() for validation and returns the\\n242:         unaltered value. This means that if to_py() returns a string rather\\n243:         than something more sophisticated, this doesn't need to be implemented.\\n244: \\n245:         Args:\\n246:             value: The original string value.\\n247: \\n248:         Return:\\n249:             The transformed value.\\n250:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n251:         self._basic_str_validation(value)\\n252: typing.Any:\\n258:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get the setting value from a config.py/YAML object.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n259:         return value\\n260: \\n261:     def to_py(self, value: typing.Any) -> typing.Any:\\n262:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get the setting value from a Python value.\\n263: \\n264:         Args:\\n265:             value: The value we got from Python/YAML.\\n266: \\n267:         Return:\\n268:             The transformed value.\\n269: \\n270:         Raise:\\n271:             configexc.ValidationError if the value was invalid.\\n272:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n273:         raise parseable again by from_str.\\n279:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n280:         if value is None:\\n281:             return ''\\n282:         assert isinstance(value, str), value\\n283:         return value\\n284: \\n285:     def to_doc(self, value: typing.Any, indent: int = 0) -> str:\\n286:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Get a string with the given value for the documentation.\\n287: \\n288:         This currently uses asciidoc syntax.\\n289:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n290:         utils.unused(indent)  # only needed for Dict/List\\n291:         str_value = self.to_str(value)\\n292:         if not str_value:\\n293:             return 'empty'\\n294:         return '+pass:[{}]+'.format(html.escape(str_value))\\n295: \\n296:     def complete(self) -> _Completions:\\n297:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Return a list of possible values for completion.\\n298: \\n299:         The default implementation just returns valid_values, but it might be\\n300:         useful to override this for special cases.\\n301: \\n302:         Return:\\n303:             A list of (value, description) tuples or None.\\n304:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n305:         if self.valid_values is None:\\n306:             return None\\n307:         else:\\n308:             out = []\\n309:             for val in self.valid_values:\\n310:                 try:\\n311:                     desc = self.valid_values.descriptions[val]\\n312:              out.append((val, desc))\\n317:             return out\\n318: \\n319: \\n320: class MappingType(BaseType):\\n321: \\n322:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Base class for any setting which has a mapping to \\n344: \\n345: class String(BaseType):\\n346: \\n347:     \\\"\\\"\\\"A string value.\\n348: \\n349:     See the setting's valid values for more information on allowed values.\\n350: \\n351: {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_016M2eVrYP1NTndVo1Gf9qvi","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01VqmWQ3RbFNYTdRbP2Yxs2g","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/app/qutebrowser/config/configtypes.py","content":"# vim: ft=python fileencoding=utf-8 sts=4 sw=4 et:\n\n# Copyright 2014-2019 Florian Bruhin (The Compiler) <mail@qutebrowser.org>\n#\n# This file is part of qutebrowser.\n#\n# qutebrowser is free software: you can redistribute it and/or modify\n# it under the terms of the GNU General Public License as published by\n# the Free Software Foundation, either version 3 of the License, or\n# (at your option) any later version.\n#\n# qutebrowser is distributed in the hope that it will be useful,\n# but WITHOUT ANY WARRANTY; without even the implied warranty of\n# MERCHANTABILITY or FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE.  See the\n# GNU General Public License for more details.\n#\n# You should have received a copy of the GNU General Public License\n# along with qutebrowser.  If not, see <http://www.gnu.org/licenses/>.\n\n\"\"\"Types for options in qutebrowser's configuration.\n\nThose types are used in configdata.yml as type of a setting.\n\nMost of them are pretty generic, but some of them are e.g. specific String\nsubclasses with valid_values set, as that particular \"type\" is used multiple\ntimes in the config.\n\nA setting value can be represented in three different ways:\n\n1) As an object which can be represented in YAML:\n   str, list, dict, int, float, True/False/None\n   This is what qutebrowser actually saves internally, and also what it gets\n   from the YAML or config.py.\n2) As a string. This is e.g. used by the :set command.\n3) As the value the code which uses it expects, e.g. enum members.\n\nConfig types can do different conversations:\n\n- Object to string with .to_str() (1 -> 2)\n- String to object with .from_str() (2 -> 1)\n- Object to code with .to_py() (1 -> 3)\n  This also validates whether the object is actually correct (type/value).\n\"\"\"\n\nimport re\nimport html\nimport codecs\nimport os.path\nimport itertools\nimport warnings\nimport datetime\nimport functools\nimport operator\nimport json\nimport typing\n\nimport attr\nimport yaml\nfrom PyQt5.QtCore import QUrl, Qt\nfrom PyQt5.QtGui import QColor, QFont\nfrom PyQt5.QtWidgets import QTabWidget, QTabBar\nfrom PyQt5.QtNetwork import QNetworkProxy\n\nfrom qutebrowser.misc import objects\nfrom qutebrowser.config import configexc, configutils\nfrom qutebrowser.utils import (standarddir, utils, qtutils, urlutils, urlmatch,\n                               usertypes)\nfrom qutebrowser.keyinput import keyutils\n\n\nclass _SystemProxy:\n\n    pass\n\n\nSYSTEM_PROXY = _SystemProxy()  # Return value for Proxy type\n\n# Taken from configparser\nBOOLEAN_STATES = {'1': True, 'yes': True, 'true': True, 'on': True,\n                  '0': False, 'no': False, 'false': False, 'off': False}\n\n\n_Completions = typing.Optional[typing.Iterable[typing.Tuple[str, str]]]\n_StrUnset = typing.Union[str, configutils.Unset]\n_StrUnsetNone = typing.Union[None, str, configutils.Unset]\n\n\nclass ValidValues:\n\n    \"\"\"Container for valid values for a given type.\n\n    Attributes:\n        values: A list with the allowed untransformed values.\n        descriptions: A dict with value/desc mappings.\n        generate_docs: Whether to show the values in the docs.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self,\n                 *values: typing.Union[str,\n                                       typing.Dict[str, str],\n                                       typing.Tuple[str, str]],\n                 generate_docs: bool = True) -> None:\n        if not values:\n            raise ValueError(\"ValidValues with no values makes no sense!\")\n        self.descriptions = {}  # type: typing.Dict[str, str]\n        self.values = []  # type: typing.List[str]\n        self.generate_docs = generate_docs\n        for value in values:\n            if isinstance(value, str):\n                # Value without description\n                self.values.append(value)\n            elif isinstance(value, dict):\n                # List of dicts from configdata.yml\n                assert len(value) == 1, value\n                value, desc = list(value.items())[0]\n                self.values.append(value)\n                self.descriptions[value] = desc\n            else:\n                # (value, description) tuple\n                self.values.append(value[0])\n                self.descriptions[value[0]] = value[1]\n\n    def __contains__(self, val: str) -> bool:\n        return val in self.values\n\n    def __iter__(self) -> typing.Iterator[str]:\n        return self.values.__iter__()\n\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:\n        return utils.get_repr(self, values=self.values,\n                              descriptions=self.descriptions)\n\n    def __eq__(self, other: object) -> bool:\n        assert isinstance(other, ValidValues)\n        return (self.values == other.values and\n                self.descriptions == other.descriptions)\n\n\nclass BaseType:\n\n    \"\"\"A type used for a setting value.\n\n    Attributes:\n        none_ok: Whether to allow None (or an empty string for :set) as value.\n\n    Class attributes:\n        valid_values: Possible values if they can be expressed as a fixed\n                      string. ValidValues instance.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n        self.none_ok = none_ok\n        self.valid_values = None  # type: typing.Optional[ValidValues]\n\n    def get_name(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get a name for the type for documentation.\"\"\"\n        return self.__class__.__name__\n\n    def get_valid_values(self) -> typing.Optional[ValidValues]:\n        \"\"\"Get the type's valid values for documentation.\"\"\"\n        return self.valid_values\n\n    def _basic_py_validation(\n            self, value: typing.Any,\n            pytype: typing.Union[type, typing.Tuple[type, ...]]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Do some basic validation for Python values (emptyness, type).\n\n        Arguments:\n            value: The value to check.\n            pytype: A Python type to check the value against.\n        \"\"\"\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return\n\n        if (value is None or (pytype == list and value == []) or\n                (pytype == dict and value == {})):\n            if not self.none_ok:\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"may not be null!\")\n            else:\n                return\n\n        if (not isinstance(value, pytype) or\n                pytype is int and isinstance(value, bool)):\n            if isinstance(pytype, tuple):\n                expected = ' or '.join(typ.__name__ for typ in pytype)\n            else:\n                expected = pytype.__name__\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                value, \"expected a value of type {} but got {}.\".format(\n                    expected, type(value).__name__))\n\n        if isinstance(value, str):\n            self._basic_str_validation(value)\n\n    def _basic_str_validation(self, value: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Do some basic validation for string values.\n\n        This checks that the value isn't empty and doesn't contain any\n        unprintable chars.\n\n        Arguments:\n            value: The value to check.\n        \"\"\"\n        assert isinstance(value, str), value\n        if not value and not self.none_ok:\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"may not be empty!\")\n        if any(ord(c) < 32 or ord(c) == 0x7f for c in value):\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                value, \"may not contain unprintable chars!\")\n\n    def _validate_surrogate_escapes(self, full_value: typing.Any,\n                                    value: typing.Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Make sure the given value doesn't contain surrogate escapes.\n\n        This is used for values passed to json.dump, as it can't handle those.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not isinstance(value, str):\n            return\n        if any(ord(c) > 0xFFFF for c in value):\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                full_value, \"may not contain surrogate escapes!\")\n\n    def _validate_valid_values(self, value: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Validate value against possible values.\n\n        The default implementation checks the value against self.valid_values\n        if it was defined.\n\n        Args:\n            value: The value to validate.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.valid_values is not None:\n            if value not in self.valid_values:\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                    value,\n                    \"valid values: {}\".format(', '.join(self.valid_values)))\n\n    def from_str(self, value: str) -> typing.Any:\n        \"\"\"Get the setting value from a string.\n\n        By default this invokes to_py() for validation and returns the\n        unaltered value. This means that if to_py() returns a string rather\n        than something more sophisticated, this doesn't need to be implemented.\n\n        Args:\n            value: The original string value.\n\n        Return:\n            The transformed value.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._basic_str_validation(value)\n        self.to_py(value)  # for validation\n        if not value:\n            return None\n        return value\n\n    def from_obj(self, value: typing.Any) -> typing.Any:\n        \"\"\"Get the setting value from a config.py/YAML object.\"\"\"\n        return value\n\n    def to_py(self, value: typing.Any) -> typing.Any:\n        \"\"\"Get the setting value from a Python value.\n\n        Args:\n            value: The value we got from Python/YAML.\n\n        Return:\n            The transformed value.\n\n        Raise:\n            configexc.ValidationError if the value was invalid.\n        \"\"\"\n        raise NotImplementedError\n\n    def to_str(self, value: typing.Any) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get a string from the setting value.\n\n        The resulting string should be parseable again by from_str.\n        \"\"\"\n        if value is None:\n completion.\n\n        The default implementation just returns valid_values, but it might be\n        useful to override this for special cases.\n\n        Return:\n            A list of (value, description) tuples or None.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.valid_values is None:\n            return None\n        else:\n            out = []\n            for val in self.valid_values:\n                try:\n                    desc = self.valid_values.descriptions[val]\n                except KeyError:\n                    # Some values are self-explaining and don't need a\n                    # description.\n                    desc = \"\"\n                out.append((val, desc))\n            return out\n\n\nclass MappingType(BaseType):\n\n    \"\"\"Base class for any setting which has a encoding.\n\n        Raises ValidationError if not.\n\n        Args:\n            value: The value to check.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.encoding is None:\n            return\n\n        try:\n configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return None\n\n        self._validate_encoding(value)\n        self._validate_valid_values(value)\n\n        if        value, \"must be {}{} or bigger!\".format(self.minval, suffix))\n        elif self.maxval is not None and value > self.maxval:\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(\n self._basic_py_validation(value, (int, str))\n        if value is None:\n            return None\n\n        if isinstance(value, str):\n            if not value.endswith('%'):\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                    value, \"needs to end with % or be an integer\")\n\n            try:\n                intval = int(value[:-1])\n            except ValueError:\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"invalid percentage!\")\n\n            if self.minperc is not None and intval < self.minperc:\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"must be {}% or \"\n                                                \"more!\".format(self.minperc))\n            if self.maxperc is not None and intval > self.maxperc:\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"must be {}% or \"\n                                                \"less!\".format(self.maxperc))\n\n            # Note we don't actually return the integer here, as we need to\n            # know whether it was a percentage.\n        else:\n            self._validate_bounds(value)\n        return value\n\n\nclass Command(BaseType):\n\n    \"\"\"A qutebrowser str) -> str:\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n        return value\n\n\nclass ColorSystem(MappingType):\n\n    \"\"\"The color system to use for color interpolation.\"\"\"\n\n    def   }\n\n\nclass QtColor(BaseType):\n\n    \"\"\"A color value.\n\n    A value can be in one of the following formats:\n\n    * `#RGB`/`#RRGGBB`/`#RRRGGGBBB`/`#RRRRGGGGBBBB`\n    * An SVG color name as specified in\n      http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG/types.html#ColorKeywords[the W3C specification].\n    * transparent (no color)\n    * `rgb(r, g, b)` / `rgba(r, g, b, a)` (values 0-255 or percentages)\n    * `hsv(h, s, v)` / `hsva(h, s, v, a)` (values 0-255, hue 0-359)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n        try:\n            return int(val)\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n\n        mult = 359.0 if kind == 'h' else 255.0\n        if val.endswith('%'):\n            val = val[:-1]\n            mult = mult / 100\n\n        try:\n            return int(float(val) * mult)\n        except ValueError:\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(val, \"must be a valid color value\")\n\n    def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> typing.Union[configutils.Unset,\n                                                      None, QColor]:\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return None\n\n        if '(' in value and value.endswith(')'):\n            openparen = value.index('(')\n            kind = value[:openparen]\n            vals = value[openparen+1:-1].split(',')\n\n            converters = {\n                'rgba': QColor.fromRgb,\n                'rgb': QColor.fromRgb,\n                'hsva': QColor.fromHsv,\n                'hsv': QColor.fromHsv,\n            }\n\n            conv = converters.get(kind)\n            if not conv:\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                    value,\n                    '{} not in {}'.format(kind, list(converters.keys())))\n\n            if len(kind) != len(vals):\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                    value,\n                    'expected {} values for {}'.format(len(kind), kind))\n\n            int_vals = [self._parse_value(p[0], p[1]) for p in zip(kind, vals)]\n            return conv(*int_vals)\n\n        color = QColor(value)\n        if color.isValid():\n            return color\n        else:\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"must be a valid color\")\n\n\nclass QssColor(BaseType):\n\n    \"\"\"A color value supporting gradients.\n\n    A value can be in one of the following formats:\n\n    * `#RGB`/`#RRGGBB`/`#RRRGGGBBB`/`#RRRRGGGGBBBB`\n `bold`, `100`..`900`\n    * Size: _number_ `px`/`pt`\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # Gets set when the config is initialized.\n    monospace_fonts = None\n    font_regex = re.compile(r\"\"\"\n        (\n            (\n                # style\n                (?P<style>normal|italic|oblique) |\n                # weight (named | 100..900)\n                (\n                    (?P<weight>[123456789]00) |\n                    (?P<namedweight>normal|bold)\n                ) |\n                # size (<float>pt | <int>px)\n                (?P<size>[0-9]+((\\\\.[0-9]+)?[pP][tT]|[pP][xX]))\n            )\\\\           # size/weight/style are space-separated\n        )*               # 0-inf size/weight/style tags\n        (?P<family>.+)  # mandatory font family\"\"\", re.VERBOSE)\n\n    def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> _StrUnsetNone:\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return None\n\n        if not self.font_regex.fullmatch(value):  # pragma: no cover\n            # This should never happen, as the regex always matches everything\n            # as family.\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"must be a valid font\")\n\n        if value.endswith(' monospace') and self.monospace_fonts is not None:\n            return value.replace('monospace', self.monospace_fonts)\n        return value\n\n\nclass FontFamily(Font):\n\n    \"\"\"A Qt font family.\"\"\"\n\n    def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> _StrUnsetNone:\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return None\n\n        match = self.font_regex.fullmatch(value)\n        if not match:  # pragma: no cover\n            # This should never happen, as the regex always matches everything\n            # as family.\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"must be a valid font\")\n        for group in 'style', 'weight', 'namedweight', 'size':\n            if match.group(group):\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"may not include a \"\n                                                \"{}!\".format(group))\n\n        return value\n\n\nclass QtFont(Font):\n\n    \"\"\"A Font which gets converted to a QFont.\"\"\"\n\n    __doc__ = Font.__doc__  # for src2asciidoc.py\n\n    def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> typing.Union[configutils.Unset,\n                                                      None, QFont]:\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return None\n\n        style_map = {\n            'normal': QFont.StyleNormal,\n            'italic': QFont.StyleItalic,\n            'oblique': QFont.StyleOblique,\n        }\n        weight_map = {\n            'normal': QFont.Normal,\n            'bold': QFont.Bold,\n        }\n        font = QFont()\n        font.setStyle(QFont.StyleNormal)\n        font.setWeight(QFont.Normal)\n\n        match = self.font_regex.fullmatch(value)\n        if not match:  # pragma: no cover\n            # This should never happen, as the regex always matches everything\n            # as family.\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"must be a valid font\")\n\n        style = match.group('style')\n        weight = match.group('weight')\n        namedweight = match.group('namedweight')\n        size = match.group('size')\n        family = match.group('family')\n        if style:\n            font.setStyle(style_map[style])\n        if namedweight:\n            font.setWeight(weight_map[namedweight])\n        if weight:\n            # based on qcssparser.cpp:setFontWeightFromValue\n            font.setWeight(min(int(weight) // 8, 99))\n        if size:\n            if size.lower().endswith('pt'):\n                font.setPointSizeF(float(size[:-2]))\n            elif size.lower().endswith('px'):\n                font.setPixelSize(int(size[:-2]))\n            else:\n                # This should never happen as the regex only lets pt/px\n                # through.\n                raise ValueError(\"Unexpected size unit in {!r}!\".format(\n                    size))  # pragma: no cover\n\n        if family == 'monospace' and self.monospace_fonts is not None:\n            family = self.monospace_fonts\n        # The Qt CSS parser handles \" and ' before passing the string to\n        # QFont.setFamily. We could do proper CSS-like parsing here, but since\n        # hopefully nobody will ever have a font with quotes in the family (if\n        # that's even possible), we take a much more naive approach.\n        family = family.replace('\"', '').replace(\"'\", '')\n        font.setFamily(family)\n\n        return font\n\n\nclass Regex(BaseType):\n\n    \"\"\"A regular expression.\n\n    When setting from `config.py`, both a string or a `re.compile(...)` object\n    are valid.\n\n    Attributes:\n        flags: The flags to be used when a string is passed.\n        _regex_type: The Python type of a regex object.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, flags: str = None,\n                 none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n        super().__init__(none_ok)\n        self._regex_type = type(re.compile(''))\n        # Parse flags from configdata.yml\n        if flags is None:\n            self.flags = 0\n        else:\n            self.flags = functools.reduce(\n                operator.or_,\n                (getattr(re, flag.strip()) for flag in flags.split(' | ')))\n\n    def _compile_regex(self, pattern: str) -> typing.Pattern[str]:\n        \"\"\"Check if the given regex is valid.\n\n        This is more complicated than it could be since there's a warning on\n        invalid escapes with newer Python versions, and we want to catch that\n        case and treat it as invalid.\n        \"\"\"\n        with warnings.catch_warnings(record=True) as recorded_warnings:\n            warnings.simplefilter('always')\n            try:\n                compiled = re.compile(pattern, self.flags)\n            except re.error as e:\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                    pattern, \"must be a valid regex - \" + str(e))\n            except RuntimeError:  # pragma: no cover\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                    pattern, \"must be a valid regex - recursion depth \"\n                    \"exceeded\")\n\n        assert recorded_warnings is not None\n\n        for w in recorded_warnings:\n            if (issubclass(w.category, DeprecationWarning) and\n                    str(w.message).startswith('bad escape')):\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                    pattern, \"must be a valid regex - \" + str(w.message))\n            else:\n                warnings.warn(w.message)\n\n        return compiled\n\n    def to_py(\n            self,\n            value: typing.Union[str, typing.Pattern[str]]\n    ) -> typing.Union[configutils.Unset, None, typing.Pattern[str]]:\n        \"\"\"Get a compiled regex from either a string or a regex object.\"\"\"\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, (str, self._regex_type))\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return None\n        elif isinstance(value, str):\n            return self._compile_regex(value)\n        else:\n            return value\n\n    def to_str(self,\n               value: typing.Union[None, str, typing.Pattern[str]]) -> str:\n        if value is None:\n            return ''\n        elif isinstance(value, self._regex_type):\n            return value.pattern\n        else:\n            assert isinstance(value, str)\n            return value\n\n\nclass Dict(BaseType):\n\n    \"\"\"A dictionary of values.\n\n    When setting from a string, pass a json-like dict, e.g. `{\"key\", \"value\"}`.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, keytype: typing.Union[String, 'Key'],\n                 valtype: BaseType, *,\n                 fixed_keys: typing.Iterable = None,\n                 required_keys: typing.Iterable = None,\n                 none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n        super().__init__(none_ok)\n        # If the keytype is not a string, we'll get problems with showing it as\n        # json in to_str() as json converts keys to strings.\n        assert isinstance(keytype, (String, Key)), keytype\n        self.keytype = keytype\n        self.valtype = valtype\n        self.fixed_keys = fixed_keys\n        self.required_keys = required_keys\n\n    def _validate_keys(self, value: typing.Dict) -> None:\n        if (self.fixed_keys is not None and not\n                set(value.keys()).issubset(self.fixed_keys)):\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                value, \"Expected keys {}\".format(self.fixed_keys))\n\n        if (self.required_keys is not None and not\n                set(self.required_keys).issubset(value.keys())):\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                value, \"Required keys {}\".format(self.required_keys))\n\n    def from_str(self, value: str) -> typing.Optional[typing.Dict]:\n        self._basic_str_validation(value)\n        if not value:\n            return None\n\n        try:\n            yaml_val = utils.yaml_load(value)\n        except yaml.YAMLError as e:\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(value, str(e))\n\n        # For the values, we actually want to call to_py, as we did parse them\n        # from YAML, so they are numbers/booleans/... already.\n        self.to_py(yaml_val)\n        return yaml_val\n\n    def from_obj(self, value: typing.Optional[typing.Dict]) -> typing.Dict:\n        if value is None:\n            return {}\n\n        return {self.keytype.from_obj(key): self.valtype.from_obj(val)\n                for key, val in value.items()}\n\n    def _fill_fixed_keys(self, value: typing.Dict) -> typing.Dict:\n        \"\"\"Fill missing fixed keys with a None-value.\"\"\"\n        if self.fixed_keys is None:\n            return value\n        for key in self.fixed_keys:\n            if key not in value:\n                value[key] = self.valtype.to_py(None)\n        return value\n\n    def to_py(\n            self,\n            value: typing.Union[typing.Dict, configutils.Unset, None]\n    ) -> typing.Union[typing.Dict, configutils.Unset]:\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, dict)\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return self._fill_fixed_keys({})\n\n        self._validate_keys(value)\n        for key, val in value.items():\n            self._validate_surrogate_escapes(value, key)\n            self._validate_surrogate_escapes(value, val)\n\n        d = {self.keytype.to_py(key): self.valtype.to_py(val)\n             for key, val in value.items()}\n        return self._fill_fixed_keys(d)\n\n    def to_str(self, value: typing.Dict) -> str:\n        if not value:\n            # An empty Dict is treated just like None -> empty string\n            return ''\n        return json.dumps(value, sort_keys=True)\n\n    def to_doc(self, value: typing.Dict, indent: int = 0) -> str:\n        if not value:\n            return 'empty'\n        lines = ['\\n']\n        prefix = '-' if not indent else '*' * indent\n        for key, val in sorted(value.items()):\n            lines += ('{} {}: {}'.format(\n                prefix,\n                self.keytype.to_doc(key),\n                self.valtype.to_doc(val, indent=indent+1),\n            )).splitlines()\n        return '\\n'.join(line.rstrip(' ') for line in lines)\n\n\nclass File(BaseType):\n\n    \"\"\"A file on the local _StrUnset) -> _StrUnsetNone:\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return This is e.g. used by the :set command.\n3) As the value the code which uses it expects, e.g. enum members.\n\nConfig types can do different conversations:\n\n- Object to string with .to_str() (1 -> 2)\n- String to object with .from_str() (2 -> 1)\n- Object to code with .to_py() (1 -> 3)\n  This also validates whether the object is actually correct (type/value).\n\"\"\"\n\nimport re\nimport html\nimport codecs\nimport os.path\nimport itertools\nimport warnings\nimport datetime\nimport functools\nimport operator\nimport json\nimport typing\n\nimport attr\nimport yaml\nfrom PyQt5.QtCore import QUrl, Qt\nfrom PyQt5.QtGui import QColor, QFont\nfrom PyQt5.QtWidgets import QTabWidget, QTabBar\nfrom PyQt5.QtNetwork import QNetworkProxy\n\nfrom qutebrowser.misc import objects\nfrom qutebrowser.config import configexc, configutils\nfrom qutebrowser.utils import (standarddir, utils, qtutils, urlutils, urlmatch,\n                               usertypes)\nfrom qutebrowser.keyinput import keyutils\n\n\nclass _SystemProxy:\n\n    pass\n\n\nSYSTEM_PROXY = _SystemProxy()  # value: The value to check.\n            pytype: A Python type to check the value against.\n        \"\"\"\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return\n\n        if (value is encoding.\n\n        Raises ValidationError if not.\n\n        Args:\n            value: The value to check.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.encoding is None:\n            return\n\n        try:\n configutils.Unset):\n            return py_value\n        elif not py_value:\n            return None\n\n        # Check for duplicate values\n        if len(set(py_value)) != len(py_value):\n actually want to call to_py, as we did parse them\n        # from YAML, so they are numbers/booleans/... already.\n        self.to_py(yaml_val)\n        return yaml_val\n\n    def from_obj(self,           value: typing.Union[typing.List, configutils.Unset]\n    ) -> typing.Union[typing.List, configutils.Unset]:\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, list)\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return []\n\n        for val in value:\n            self._validate_surrogate_escapes(value, val)\n\n        if self.length is not None and len(value) != self.length:\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"Exactly {} values need to \"\n                                            \"be set!\".format(self.length))\n        return [self.valtype.to_py(v) for v in value]\n\n    def to_str(self, value: typing.List) -> str:\n        if not value:\n            # An empty list is treated just like None -> empty string\n            return ''\n        return json.dumps(value)\n\n    def to_doc(self, value: typing.List, indent: int = 0) -> str:\n        if not value:\n            return 'empty'\n\n        # Might work, but untested\n        assert not isinstance(self.valtype, (Dict, List)), self.valtype\n\n        lines = ['\\n']\n        prefix = '-' if not indent else '*' * indent\n        for elem in value:\n            lines.append('{} {}'.format(\n                prefix,\n                self.valtype.to_doc(elem, indent=indent+1)))\n        return '\\n'.join(lines)\n\n\nclass ListOrValue(BaseType):\n\n    \"\"\"A list of values, or a single value.\n\n    //\n\n    Internally, the value is stored as either a value (of valtype), or a list.\n for to_str/to_doc/from_str.\"\"\"\n        if isinstance(value, list):\n            if len(value) == 1:\n                return value[0], self.valtype\n            else:\n                return value, self.listtype\n        else:\n            return value, self.valtype\n\n    def get_name(self) -> str:\n        return self.listtype.get_name() + ', or ' + self.valtype.get_name()\n\n    def duplicate flags are invalid. Each item is checked against\n    the valid values of the setting.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    combinable_values = None  # type: typing.Optional[typing.Sequence]\n\n    _show_valtype = False\n\n    def vals\n\n    def complete(self) -> _Completions:\n        valid_values = self.valtype.valid_values\n        if valid_values is None:\n            return None\n\n        out = []\n        # Single value completions\n        for value in valid_values:\n            desc = valid_values.descriptions.get(value, \"\")\n            out.append((json.dumps([value]), desc))\n\n        combinables = self.combinable_values\n        if combinables is None:\n            combinables = list(valid_values)\n        # Generate combinations of each possible value combination\n        for size in range(2, len(combinables) + 1):\n            for combination in itertools.combinations(combinables, size):\n                out.append((json.dumps(combination), ''))\n        return out\n\n\nclass disable=abstract-method\n\n    \"\"\"Base class for Float/Int.\n\n    Attributes:\n        minval: Minimum value (inclusive).\n        maxval: Maximum value (inclusive).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def self._parse_bound(minval)\n        self.maxval = self._parse_bound(maxval)\n        if self.maxval is not None and self.minval is not None:\n            if self.maxval < self.minval:\n                raise ValueError(\"minval ({}) needs to be <= maxval ({})!\"\n                                 .format(self.minval, self.maxval))\n\n    def _parse_bound(\n            self, bound: typing.Union[None, str, int, float]\n    ) -> typing.Union[None, int, float]:\n        \"\"\"Get a numeric bound from a string like 'maxint'.\"\"\"\n        if bound == 'maxint':\n            return qtutils.MAXVALS['int']\n        elif bound == 'maxint64':\n            return qtutils.MAXVALS['int64']\n        else:\n            if bound is not None:\n                assert isinstance(bound, (int, float)), bound\n            return bound\n\n    def _validate_bounds(self, value: typing.Union[None, int, float],\n                         suffix: str = '') -> None:\n        \"\"\"Validate self.minval and self.maxval.\"\"\"\n        if value is None:\n            return\n        elif self.minval is not None and value < self.minval:\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                value, \"must be {}{} or bigger!\".format(self.minval, suffix))\n        elif self.maxval is not None and value > self.maxval:\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                value, \"must be {}{} or smaller!\".format(self.maxval, suffix))\n\n    def to_str(self, value: typing.Union[None, int, float]) -> str:\n        if value is None:\n            return ''\n maxperc: Maximum value for percentage (inclusive).\n        minint: Minimum value for integer (inclusive).\n        maxint: Maximum value for integer (inclusive).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, self,\n            value: typing.Union[None, str, int]\n    ) -> typing.Union[None, str, int]:\n        \"\"\"Expect a value like '42%' as string, or 23 as int.\"\"\"\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, (int, str))\n        if value is None:\n            return None\n\n        if isinstance(value, str):\n            if not value.endswith('%'):\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                    value, \"needs to end with % or be an integer\")\n\n            try:\n                intval = int(value[:-1])\n            except ValueError:\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"invalid percentage!\")\n\n            if self.minperc is not None and intval < self.minperc:\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(value,
