specified in\n      http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG/types.html#ColorKeywords[the W3C specification].\n    * transparent (no color)\n    * `rgb(r, g, b)` / `rgba(r, g, b, a)` (values 0-255 or percentages)\n    * `hsv(h, s, v)` / `hsva(h, s, v, a)` (values 0-255, hue 0-359)\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n        try:\n            return int(val)\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n\n        mult = 359.0 if kind == 'h' else 255.0\n        if val.endswith('%'):\n            val = val[:-1]\n            mult = mult / 100\n\n        try:\n            return int(float(val) * mult)\n        except ValueError:\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(val, \"must be a valid color value\")\n\n    def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> typing.Union[configutils.Unset,\n                                                      None, QColor]:\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return None\n\n        if '(' in value and value.endswith(')'):\n            openparen = value.index('(')\n            kind = value[:openparen]\n            vals = value[openparen+1:-1].split(',')\n\n            converters = {\n                'rgba': QColor.fromRgb,\n                'rgb': QColor.fromRgb,\n                'hsva': QColor.fromHsv,\n                'hsv': QColor.fromHsv,\n            }\n\n            conv = converters.get(kind)\n            if not conv:\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                    value,\n                    '{} not in {}'.format(kind, list(converters.keys())))\n\n            if len(kind) != len(vals):\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                    value,\n                    'expected {} values for {}'.format(len(kind), kind))\n\n            int_vals = [self._parse_value(p[0], p[1]) for p in zip(kind, vals)]\n            return conv(*int_vals)\n\n        color = QColor(value)\n        if color.isValid():\n            return color\n        else:\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"must be a valid color\")\n\n\nclass QssColor(BaseType):\n\n    \"\"\"A color value supporting gradients.\n\n    A value can be in one of the following formats:\n\n    * `#RGB`/`#RRGGBB`/`#RRRGGGBBB`/`#RRRRGGGGBBBB`\n    * An SVG color name as specified in\n      http://www.w3.org/TR/SVG/types.html#ColorKeywords[the W3C specification].\n    * transparent (no color)\n    * `rgb(r, g, b)` / `rgba(r, g, b, a)` (values 0-255 or percentages)\n    * `hsv(h, s, v)` / `hsva(h, s, v, a)` (values 0-255, hue 0-359)\n    * A gradient as explained in\n      http://doc.qt.io/qt-5/stylesheet-reference.html#list-of-property-types[the Qt documentation]\n      under ``Gradient''\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> _StrUnsetNone:\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return None\n\n        functions = ['rgb', 'rgba', 'hsv', 'hsva', 'qlineargradient',\n                     'qradialgradient', 'qconicalgradient']\n        if (any(value.startswith(func + '(') for func in functions) and\n                value.endswith(')')):\n            # QColor doesn't handle these\n            return value\n\n        if not QColor.isValidColor(value):\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"must be a valid color\")\n\n        return value\n\n\nclass Font(BaseType):\n\n    \"\"\"A font family, with optional style/weight/size.\n\n    * Style: `normal`/`italic`/`oblique`\n    * Weight: `normal`, `bold`, `100`..`900`\n    * Size: _number_ `px`/`pt`\n    \"\"\"\n\n    # Gets set when the config is initialized.\n    monospace_fonts = None\n    font_regex = re.compile(r\"\"\"\n        (\n            (\n                # style\n                (?P<style>normal|italic|oblique) |\n                # weight (named | 100..900)\n                (\n                    (?P<weight>[123456789]00) |\n                    (?P<namedweight>normal|bold)\n                ) |\n                # size (<float>pt | <int>px)\n                (?P<size>[0-9]+((\\\\.[0-9]+)?[pP][tT]|[pP][xX]))\n            )\\\\           # size/weight/style are space-separated\n        )*               # 0-inf size/weight/style tags\n        (?P<family>.+)  # mandatory font family\"\"\", re.VERBOSE)\n\n    def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> _StrUnsetNone:\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return None\n\n        if not self.font_regex.fullmatch(value):  # pragma: no cover\n            # This should never happen, as the regex always matches everything\n            # as family.\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"must be a valid font\")\n\n        if value.endswith(' monospace') and self.monospace_fonts is not None:\n            return value.replace('monospace', self.monospace_fonts)\n        return value\n\n\nclass FontFamily(Font):\n\n    \"\"\"A Qt font family.\"\"\"\n\n    def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> _StrUnsetNone:\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return None\n\n        match = self.font_regex.fullmatch(value)\n        if not match:  # pragma: no cover\n            # This should never happen, as the regex always matches everything\n            # as family.\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"must be a valid font\")\n        for group in 'style', 'weight', 'namedweight', 'size':\n            if match.group(group):\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"may not include a \"\n                                                \"{}!\".format(group))\n\n        return value\n\n\nclass QtFont(Font):\n\n    \"\"\"A Font which gets converted to a QFont.\"\"\"\n\n    __doc__ = Font.__doc__  # for src2asciidoc.py\n\n    def to_py(self, value: _StrUnset) -> typing.Union[configutils.Unset,\n                                                      None, QFont]:\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, str)\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return None\n\n        style_map = {\n            'normal': QFont.StyleNormal,\n            'italic': QFont.StyleItalic,\n            'oblique': QFont.StyleOblique,\n        }\n        weight_map = {\n            'normal': QFont.Normal,\n            'bold': QFont.Bold,\n        }\n        font = QFont()\n        font.setStyle(QFont.StyleNormal)\n        font.setWeight(QFont.Normal)\n\n        match = self.font_regex.fullmatch(value)\n        if not match:  # pragma: no cover\n            # This should never happen, as the regex always matches everything\n            # as family.\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(value, \"must be a valid font\")\n\n        style = match.group('style')\n        weight = match.group('weight')\n        namedweight = match.group('namedweight')\n        size = match.group('size')\n        family = match.group('family')\n        if style:\n            font.setStyle(style_map[style])\n        if namedweight:\n            font.setWeight(weight_map[namedweight])\n        if weight:\n            # based on qcssparser.cpp:setFontWeightFromValue\n            font.setWeight(min(int(weight) // 8, 99))\n        if size:\n            if size.lower().endswith('pt'):\n                font.setPointSizeF(float(size[:-2]))\n            elif size.lower().endswith('px'):\n                font.setPixelSize(int(size[:-2]))\n            else:\n                # This should never happen as the regex only lets pt/px\n                # through.\n                raise ValueError(\"Unexpected size unit in {!r}!\".format(\n                    size))  # pragma: no cover\n\n        if family == 'monospace' and self.monospace_fonts is not None:\n            family = self.monospace_fonts\n        # The Qt CSS parser handles \" and ' before passing the string to\n        # QFont.setFamily. We could do proper CSS-like parsing here, but since\n        # hopefully nobody will ever have a font with quotes in the family (if\n        # that's even possible), we take a much more naive approach.\n        family = family.replace('\"', '').replace(\"'\", '')\n        font.setFamily(family)\n\n        return font\n\n\nclass Regex(BaseType):\n\n    \"\"\"A regular expression.\n\n    When setting from `config.py`, both a string or a `re.compile(...)` object\n    are valid.\n\n    Attributes:\n        flags: The flags to be used when a string is passed.\n        _regex_type: The Python type of a regex object.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, flags: str = None,\n                 none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n        super().__init__(none_ok)\n        self._regex_type = type(re.compile(''))\n        # Parse flags from configdata.yml\n        if flags is None:\n            self.flags = 0\n        else:\n            self.flags = functools.reduce(\n                operator.or_,\n                (getattr(re, flag.strip()) for flag in flags.split(' | ')))\n\n    def _compile_regex(self, pattern: str) -> typing.Pattern[str]:\n        \"\"\"Check if the given regex is valid.\n\n        This is more complicated than it could be since there's a warning on\n        invalid escapes with newer Python versions, and we want to catch that\n        case and treat it as invalid.\n        \"\"\"\n        with warnings.catch_warnings(record=True) as recorded_warnings:\n            warnings.simplefilter('always')\n            try:\n                compiled = re.compile(pattern, self.flags)\n            except re.error as e:\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                    pattern, \"must be a valid regex - \" + str(e))\n            except RuntimeError:  # pragma: no cover\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                    pattern, \"must be a valid regex - recursion depth \"\n                    \"exceeded\")\n\n        assert recorded_warnings is not None\n\n        for w in recorded_warnings:\n            if (issubclass(w.category, DeprecationWarning) and\n                    str(w.message).startswith('bad escape')):\n                raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                    pattern, \"must be a valid regex - \" + str(w.message))\n            else:\n                warnings.warn(w.message)\n\n        return compiled\n\n    def to_py(\n            self,\n            value: typing.Union[str, typing.Pattern[str]]\n    ) -> typing.Union[configutils.Unset, None, typing.Pattern[str]]:\n        \"\"\"Get a compiled regex from either a string or a regex object.\"\"\"\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, (str, self._regex_type))\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return None\n        elif isinstance(value, str):\n            return self._compile_regex(value)\n        else:\n            return value\n\n    def to_str(self,\n               value: typing.Union[None, str, typing.Pattern[str]]) -> str:\n        if value is None:\n            return ''\n        elif isinstance(value, self._regex_type):\n            return value.pattern\n        else:\n            assert isinstance(value, str)\n            return value\n\n\nclass Dict(BaseType):\n\n    \"\"\"A dictionary of values.\n\n    When setting from a string, pass a json-like dict, e.g. `{\"key\", \"value\"}`.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, keytype: typing.Union[String, 'Key'],\n                 valtype: BaseType, *,\n                 fixed_keys: typing.Iterable = None,\n                 required_keys: typing.Iterable = None,\n                 none_ok: bool = False) -> None:\n        super().__init__(none_ok)\n        # If the keytype is not a string, we'll get problems with showing it as\n        # json in to_str() as json converts keys to strings.\n        assert isinstance(keytype, (String, Key)), keytype\n        self.keytype = keytype\n        self.valtype = valtype\n        self.fixed_keys = fixed_keys\n        self.required_keys = required_keys\n\n    def _validate_keys(self, value: typing.Dict) -> None:\n        if (self.fixed_keys is not None and not\n                set(value.keys()).issubset(self.fixed_keys)):\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                value, \"Expected keys {}\".format(self.fixed_keys))\n\n        if (self.required_keys is not None and not\n                set(self.required_keys).issubset(value.keys())):\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(\n                value, \"Required keys {}\".format(self.required_keys))\n\n    def from_str(self, value: str) -> typing.Optional[typing.Dict]:\n        self._basic_str_validation(value)\n        if not value:\n            return None\n\n        try:\n            yaml_val = utils.yaml_load(value)\n        except yaml.YAMLError as e:\n            raise configexc.ValidationError(value, str(e))\n\n        # For the values, we actually want to call to_py, as we did parse them\n        # from YAML, so they are numbers/booleans/... already.\n        self.to_py(yaml_val)\n        return yaml_val\n\n    def from_obj(self, value: typing.Optional[typing.Dict]) -> typing.Dict:\n        if value is None:\n            return {}\n\n        return {self.keytype.from_obj(key): self.valtype.from_obj(val)\n                for key, val in value.items()}\n\n    def _fill_fixed_keys(self, value: typing.Dict) -> typing.Dict:\n        \"\"\"Fill missing fixed keys with a None-value.\"\"\"\n        if self.fixed_keys is None:\n            return value\n        for key in self.fixed_keys:\n            if key not in value:\n                value[key] = self.valtype.to_py(None)\n        return value\n\n    def to_py(\n            self,\n            value: typing.Union[typing.Dict, configutils.Unset, None]\n    ) -> typing.Union[typing.Dict, configutils.Unset]:\n        self._basic_py_validation(value, dict)\n        if isinstance(value, configutils.Unset):\n            return value\n        elif not value:\n            return self._fill_fixed_keys({})\n\n        self._validate_keys(value)\n        for key, val in value.items():\n            self._validate_surrogate_escapes(value, key)\n            self._validate_surrogate_escapes(value, val)\n\n        d = {self.keytype.to_py(key): self.valtype.to_py(val)\n             for key, val in value.items()}\n        return self._fill_fixed_keys(d)\n\n    def to_str(self, value: typing.Dict) -> str:\n        if not value:\n            # An empty Dict is treated just like None -> empty string\n            return ''\n        return json.dumps(value, sort_keys=True)\n\n    def to_doc(self, value: typing.Dict, indent: int = 0) -> str:\n        if not value:\n            return 'empty'\n        lines = ['\\n']\n        prefix = '-' if not indent else '*' * indent\n        for key, val in sorted(value.items()):\n            lines += ('{} {}: {}'.format(\n                prefix,\n                self.keytype.to_doc(key),\n                self.valtype.to_doc(val, indent=indent+1),\n            )).splitlines()\n        return '\\n'.join(line.rstrip(' ') for line in lines)\n\n\nclass File(BaseType):\n\n    \"\"\"A file on the local {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01BaoggUdgxexmZs29JUbaPf","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01LN6V2wgHLGX1njGGnuDsHw","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/regression_test.py","content":"\"\"\"Regression test for HSV color percentage parsing bug.\n\nThis test verifies that HSV colors with percentage values are parsed correctly,\nwith hue scaling to 0-359 degrees instead of 0-255.\n\nThe bug is that `hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)` is parsed as `(255, 255, 255)` instead\nof `(359, 255, 255)`. This affects both HSV and HSVA color parsing.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport pytest\n\n\nclass MockBuggyHSVParser:\n    \"\"\"Mock implementation of the buggy HSV parser (uses 255 for all channels).\"\"\"\n\n    def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Parse a single HSV/HSVA value - BUGGY VERSION.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return int(val)\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n\n        # BUG: Always use 255 instead of 359 for hue\n        mult = 255.0  # BUGGY - should be 359.0 for hue\n        if val.endswith('%'):\n            val = val[:-1]\n            mult = mult / 100\n\n        try:\n            return int(float(val) * mult)\n        except ValueError:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid color value: {val}\")\n\n    def parse_hsv(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSV color string and return (h, s, v) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)  # h parameter for hue\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)  # s parameter for saturation\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)  # v parameter for value\n        return (h, s, v)\n\n    def parse_hsva(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str, a_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSVA color string and return (h, s, v, a) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)\n        a = self._parse_value('a', a_str)\n        return (h, s, v, a)\n\n\nclass MockCorrectHSVParser:\n    \"\"\"Mock implementation of the correct HSV parser.\"\"\"\n\n    def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Parse a single HSV/HSVA value - CORRECT VERSION.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return int(val)\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n\n        # CORRECT: Use 359 for hue, 255 for other channels\n        mult = 359.0 if kind == 'h' else 255.0\n        if val.endswith('%'):\n            val = val[:-1]\n            mult = mult / 100\n\n        try:\n            return int(float(val) * mult)\n        except ValueError:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid color value: {val}\")\n\n    def parse_hsv(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSV color string and return (h, s, v) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)  # h parameter for hue\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)  # s parameter for saturation\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)  # v parameter for value\n        return (h, s, v)\n\n    def parse_hsva(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str, a_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSVA color string and return (h, s, v, a) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)\n        a = self._parse_value('a', a_str)\n        return (h, s, v, a)\n\n\nclass TestHSVBugDemonstration:\n    \"\"\"Tests that demonstrate the HSV hue scaling bug vs correct behavior.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_bug_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 100% hue should be 359, not 255.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Parse 100% hue with the buggy implementation\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # Parse 100% hue with the correct implementation\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # The bug: hue is scaled to 255 instead of 359\n        assert h_buggy == 255, f\"Buggy parser should give h=255, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 359, f\"Correct parser should give h=359, got {h_correct}\"\n\n        # Saturation and value should be the same in both\n        assert s_buggy == 255\n        assert s_correct == 255\n        assert v_buggy == 255\n        assert v_correct == 255\n\n    def test_bug_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 50% hue should be ~179, not ~127.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Parse 50% hue\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('50%', '50%', '50%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('50%', '50%', '50%')\n\n        # The bug: 50% of 255 = 127.5 → 127\n        # Correct: 50% of 359 = 179.5 → 179\n        assert h_buggy == 127, f\"Buggy parser should give h=127 for 50%, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 179, f\"Correct parser should give h=179 for 50%, got {h_correct}\"\n\n    def test_bug_25_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 25% hue should be ~89-90, not ~63-64.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv('25%', '25%', '25%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv('25%', '25%', '25%')\n\n        # 25% of 255 = 63.75 → 63 (buggy)\n        # 25% of 359 = 89.75 → 89 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 63, f\"Buggy: 25% hue should be 63, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 89, f\"Correct: 25% hue should be 89, got {h_correct}\"\n\n    def test_bug_10_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 10% hue should be ~35, not ~25.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('10%', '10%', '10%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('10%', '10%', '10%')\n\n        # 10% of 255 = 25.5 → 25 (buggy)\n        # 10% of 359 = 35.9 → 35 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 25, f\"Buggy: 10% hue should be 25, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 35, f\"Correct: 10% hue should be 35, got {h_correct}\"\n\n        # Both should have same saturation/value since both use 255\n        assert s_buggy == 25 or s_buggy == 26  # int(10% * 255) = int(25.5)\n        assert v_buggy == 25 or v_buggy == 26\n        assert s_correct == 25 or s_correct == 26\n        assert v_correct == 25 or v_correct == 26\n\n\nclass TestHSVABugDemonstration:\n    \"\"\"Tests that demonstrate the HSVA (with alpha) hue scaling bug.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_hsva_bug_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: HSVA 100% hue should be 359, not 255.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy, a_buggy = buggy.parse_hsva('100%', '100%', '100%', '100%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct, a_correct = correct.parse_hsva('100%', '100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # Hue should differ: buggy uses 255, correct uses 359\n        assert h_buggy == 255\n        assert h_correct == 359\n\n        # All other channels should be the same\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 255\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 255\n        assert a_buggy == a_correct == 255\n\n    def test_hsva_bug_50_percent(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: HSVA 50% values with hue scaling difference.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy, a_buggy = buggy.parse_hsva('50%', '50%', '50%', '50%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct, a_correct = correct.parse_hsva('50%', '50%', '50%', '50%')\n\n        # Hue differs: 50% of 255 vs 50% of 359\n        assert h_buggy == 127\n        assert h_correct == 179\n\n        # S, V, A should all be the same (all use 255)\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 127 or s_correct == 128\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 127 or v_correct == 128\n        assert a_buggy == a_correct == 127 or a_correct == 128\n\n\nclass TestIntegerValuesUnaffected:\n    \"\"\"Test that integer HSV values are not affected by the bug.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_integer_hue_same_in_both(self):\n        \"\"\"Integer hue values should work the same in buggy and correct.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # This affects both HSV and HSVA color parsing.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport pytest\n\n\nclass MockBuggyHSVParser:\n    \"\"\"Mock implementation of the buggy HSV parser (uses 255 for all channels).\"\"\"\n\n    def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Parse a single HSV/HSVA value - BUGGY VERSION.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return int(val)\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n\n        # BUG: Always use 255 instead of 359 for hue\n        mult = 255.0  # BUGGY - should be 359.0 for hue\n        if val.endswith('%'):\n            val = val[:-1]\n            mult = mult / 100\n\n        try:\n            return int(float(val) * mult)\n        except ValueError:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid color value: {val}\")\n\n    def parse_hsv(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSV color string and return (h, s, v) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)  # h parameter for hue\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)  # s parameter for saturation\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)  # v parameter for value\n        return (h, s, v)\n\n    def parse_hsva(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str, a_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSVA color string and return (h, s, v, a) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)\n        a = self._parse_value('a', a_str)\n        return (h, s, v, a)\n\n\nclass MockCorrectHSVParser:\n    \"\"\"Mock implementation of the correct HSV parser.\"\"\"\n\n    def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Parse a single HSV/HSVA value - CORRECT VERSION.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return int(val)\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n\n        # CORRECT: Use 359 for hue, 255 for other channels\n        mult = 359.0 if kind == 'h' else 255.0\n        if val.endswith('%'):\n            val = val[:-1]\n            mult = mult / 100\n\n        try:\n            return int(float(val) * mult)\n        except ValueError:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid color value: {val}\")\n\n    def parse_hsv(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSV color string and return (h, s, v) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)  # h parameter for hue\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)  # s parameter for saturation\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)  # v parameter for value\n        return (h, s, v)\n\n    def parse_hsva(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str, a_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSVA color string and return (h, s, v, a) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)\n        a = self._parse_value('a', a_str)\n        return (h, s, v, a)\n\n\nclass TestHSVBugDemonstration:\n    \"\"\"Tests that demonstrate the HSV hue scaling bug vs correct behavior.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_bug_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 100% hue should be 359, not 255.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Parse 100% hue with the buggy implementation\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # Parse 100% hue with the correct implementation\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # The bug: hue is scaled to 255 instead of 359\n        assert h_buggy == 255, f\"Buggy parser should give h=255, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 359, f\"Correct parser should give h=359, got {h_correct}\"\n\n        # Saturation and value should be the same in both\n        assert s_buggy == 255\n        assert s_correct == 255\n        assert v_buggy == 255\n        assert v_correct == 255\n\n    def test_bug_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 50% hue should be ~179, not ~127.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # 0% should always be 0","+        ('10%', 25, 35),        # Off by 10 degrees","+        ('25%', 63, 89),        # Off by 26 degrees","+        ('33%', 84, 118),       # Off by 34 degrees","+        ('50%', 127, 179),      # Off by 52 degrees","+        ('66%', 168, 236),      # Off by 68 degrees","+        ('75%', 191, 269),      # Off by 78 degrees","+        ('90%', 229, 323),      # Off by 94 degrees","+        ('100%', 255, 359),     # Off by 104 degrees","+    ])","+    def test_hue_percentage_scaling_error(self, percent, buggy_expected, correct_expected):","+        \"\"\"Parametrized test showing hue scaling error for various percentages.\"\"\"","+        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()","+        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()","+","+        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv(percent, '50%', '50%')","+        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv(percent, '50%', '50%')","+","+        assert h_buggy == buggy_expected, \\","+            f\"Buggy: {percent} hue should be {buggy_expected}, got {h_buggy}\"","+        assert h_correct == correct_expected, \\","+            f\"Correct: {percent} hue should be {correct_expected}, got {h_correct}\"","+","+        # Verify they differ","+        assert h_buggy != h_correct, \\","+            f\"Bug not present: buggy and correct should differ for {percent}\"","+","+"," if __name__ == '__main__':","     pytest.main([__file__, '-v', '--tb=short'])"]}],"originalFile":"\"\"\"Regression test for HSV color percentage parsing bug.\n\nThis test verifies that HSV colors with percentage values are parsed correctly,\nwith hue scaling to 0-359 degrees instead of 0-255.\n\nThe bug is that `hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)` is parsed as `(255, 255, 255)` instead\nof `(359, 255, 255)`. This affects both HSV and HSVA color parsing.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport sys\nimport os\n\n# Add the qutebrowser directory to the path so we can import modules\nsys.path.insert(0, '/app')\n\nimport pytest\nfrom PyQt5.QtGui import QColor\n\n# Import the color type handler\nfrom qutebrowser.config.configtypes import QtColor\n\n\nclass TestHSVPercentageScaling:\n    \"\"\"Test that HSV colors with percentages scale hue correctly.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_hsv_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 100% hue maps to 359 degrees, not 255.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 100% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(100%, 100%, 100%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # With the bug, hue would be 255 (100% of 255)\n        # With the fix, hue should be 359 (100% of 359)\n        assert h == 359, f\"Expected hue=359 for 100%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 255, f\"Expected saturation=255, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 255, f\"Expected value=255, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 50% hue maps to ~179 degrees, not ~127.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 50% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(50%, 50%, 50%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # With the bug, hue would be 127 (50% of 255)\n        # With the fix, hue should be 179 (50% of 359)\n        assert h == 179, f\"Expected hue=179 for 50%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 127 or s == 128, f\"Expected saturation~127, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 127 or v == 128, f\"Expected value~127, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_10_percent(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 10% scales correctly.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 10% values\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(10%, 10%, 10%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # 10% of 359 ≈ 35.9 → 35\n        # 10% of 255 ≈ 25.5 → 25\n        assert h == 35, f\"Expected hue=35 for 10%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 25 or s == 26, f\"Expected saturation~25, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 25 or v == 26, f\"Expected value~25, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsva_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that HSVA (with alpha) also scales hue correctly.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSVA color with 100% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsva(100%, 100%, 100%, 100%)')\n\n        # Get the HSVA components\n        h, s, v, a = result.getHsv()\n\n        # With the bug, hue would be 255\n        # With the fix, hue should be 359\n        assert h == 359, f\"Expected hue=359 for 100%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 255, f\"Expected saturation=255, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 255, f\"Expected value=255, got {v}\"\n        assert a == 255, f\"Expected alpha=255, got {a}\"\n\n    def test_hsva_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that HSVA with 50% hue scales correctly.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSVA color with 50% values\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsva(50%, 50%, 50%, 50%)')\n\n        # Get the HSVA components\n        h, s, v, a = result.getHsv()\n\n        # 50% of 359 ≈ 179.5 → 179\n        # 50% of 255 ≈ 127.5 → 127\n        assert h == 179, f\"Expected hue=179 for 50%, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 127 or s == 128, f\"Expected saturation~127, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 127 or v == 128, f\"Expected value~127, got {v}\"\n        assert a == 127 or a == 128, f\"Expected alpha~127, got {a}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_25_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that 25% hue maps to ~89-90 degrees, not ~63-64.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with 25% hue\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(25%, 25%, 25%)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # 25% of 359 ≈ 89.75 → 89\n        # 25% of 255 (buggy) ≈ 63.75 → 63\n        assert h == 89, f\"Expected hue=89 for 25%, got {h}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_integer_values_unchanged(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that integer HSV values work as expected.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSV color with integer values (0-359 for hue, 0-255 for s/v)\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(180, 128, 64)')\n\n        # Get the HSV components\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # Integer values should be used directly\n        assert h == 180, f\"Expected hue=180, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 128, f\"Expected saturation=128, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 64, f\"Expected value=64, got {v}\"\n\n    def test_hsva_integer_values_unchanged(self):\n        \"\"\"Test that integer HSVA values work as expected.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # Parse an HSVA color with integer values\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsva(180, 128, 64, 200)')\n\n        # Get the HSVA components\n        h, s, v, a = result.getHsv()\n\n        # Integer values should be used directly\n        assert h == 180, f\"Expected hue=180, got {h}\"\n        assert s == 128, f\"Expected saturation=128, got {s}\"\n        assert v == 64, f\"Expected value=64, got {v}\"\n        assert a == 200, f\"Expected alpha=200, got {a}\"\n\n    def test_hsv_mixed_percentage_and_integer(self):\n        \"\"\"Test HSV with mixed percentage and integer values.\"\"\"\n        color_type = QtColor()\n\n        # This should fail with a validation error since all values must be\n        # either percentages or integers, not mixed\n        # But let's test with all percentages for correctness\n        result = color_type.to_py('hsv(25%, 64, 32)')\n        h, s, v, _ = result.getHsv()\n\n        # This affects both HSV and HSVA color parsing.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport pytest\n\n\nclass MockBuggyHSVParser:\n    \"\"\"Mock implementation of the buggy HSV parser (uses 255 for all channels).\"\"\"\n\n    def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Parse a single HSV/HSVA value - BUGGY VERSION.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return int(val)\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n\n        # BUG: Always use 255 instead of 359 for hue\n        mult = 255.0  # BUGGY - should be 359.0 for hue\n        if val.endswith('%'):\n            val = val[:-1]\n            mult = mult / 100\n\n        try:\n            return int(float(val) * mult)\n        except ValueError:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid color value: {val}\")\n\n    def parse_hsv(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSV color string and return (h, s, v) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)  # h parameter for hue\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)  # s parameter for saturation\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)  # v parameter for value\n        return (h, s, v)\n\n    def parse_hsva(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str, a_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSVA color string and return (h, s, v, a) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)\n        a = self._parse_value('a', a_str)\n        return (h, s, v, a)\n\n\nclass MockCorrectHSVParser:\n    \"\"\"Mock implementation of the correct HSV parser.\"\"\"\n\n    def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Parse a single HSV/HSVA value - CORRECT VERSION.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return int(val)\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n\n        # CORRECT: Use 359 for hue, 255 for other channels\n        mult = 359.0 if kind == 'h' else 255.0\n        if val.endswith('%'):\n            val = val[:-1]\n            mult = mult / 100\n\n        try:\n            return int(float(val) * mult)\n        except ValueError:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid color value: {val}\")\n\n    def parse_hsv(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSV color string and return (h, s, v) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)  # h parameter for hue\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)  # s parameter for saturation\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)  # v parameter for value\n        return (h, s, v)\n\n    def parse_hsva(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str, a_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSVA color string and return (h, s, v, a) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)\n        a = self._parse_value('a', a_str)\n        return (h, s, v, a)\n\n\nclass TestHSVBugDemonstration:\n    \"\"\"Tests that demonstrate the HSV hue scaling bug vs correct behavior.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_bug_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 100% hue should be 359, not 255.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Parse 100% hue with the buggy implementation\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # Parse 100% hue with the correct implementation\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # The bug: hue is scaled to 255 instead of 359\n        assert h_buggy == 255, f\"Buggy parser should give h=255, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 359, f\"Correct parser should give h=359, got {h_correct}\"\n\n        # Saturation and value should be the same in both\n        assert s_buggy == 255\n        assert s_correct == 255\n        assert v_buggy == 255\n        assert v_correct == 255\n\n    def test_bug_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 50% hue should be ~179, not ~127.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Parse 50% hue\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('50%', '50%', '50%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('50%', '50%', '50%')\n\n        # The bug: 50% of 255 = 127.5 → 127\n        # Correct: 50% of 359 = 179.5 → 179\n        assert h_buggy == 127, f\"Buggy parser should give h=127 for 50%, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 179, f\"Correct parser should give h=179 for 50%, got {h_correct}\"\n\n    def test_bug_25_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 25% hue should be ~89-90, not ~63-64.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv('25%', '25%', '25%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv('25%', '25%', '25%')\n\n        # 25% of 255 = 63.75 → 63 (buggy)\n        # 25% of 359 = 89.75 → 89 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 63, f\"Buggy: 25% hue should be 63, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 89, f\"Correct: 25% hue should be 89, got {h_correct}\"\n\n    def test_bug_10_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 10% hue should be ~35, not ~25.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('10%', '10%', '10%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('10%', '10%', '10%')\n\n        # 10% of 255 = 25.5 → 25 (buggy)\n        # 10% of 359 = 35.9 → 35 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 25, f\"Buggy: 10% hue should be 25, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 35, f\"Correct: 10% hue should be 35, got {h_correct}\"\n\n        # Both should have same saturation/value since both use 255\n        assert s_buggy == 25 or s_buggy == 26  # int(10% * 255) = int(25.5)\n        assert v_buggy == 25 or v_buggy == 26\n        assert s_correct == 25 or s_correct == 26\n        assert v_correct == 25 or v_correct == 26\n\n\nclass TestHSVABugDemonstration:\n    \"\"\"Tests that demonstrate the HSVA (with alpha) hue scaling bug.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_hsva_bug_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: HSVA 100% hue should be 359, not 255.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy, a_buggy = buggy.parse_hsva('100%', '100%', '100%', '100%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct, a_correct = correct.parse_hsva('100%', '100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # Hue should differ: buggy uses 255, correct uses 359\n        assert h_buggy == 255\n        assert h_correct == 359\n\n        # All other channels should be the same\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 255\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 255\n        assert a_buggy == a_correct == 255\n\n    def test_hsva_bug_50_percent(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: HSVA 50% values with hue scaling difference.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy, a_buggy = buggy.parse_hsva('50%', '50%', '50%', '50%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct, a_correct = correct.parse_hsva('50%', '50%', '50%', '50%')\n\n        # Hue differs: 50% of 255 vs 50% of 359\n        assert h_buggy == 127\n        assert h_correct == 179\n\n        # S, V, A should all be the same (all use 255)\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 127 or s_correct == 128\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 127 or v_correct == 128\n        assert a_buggy == a_correct == 127 or a_correct == 128\n\n\nclass TestIntegerValuesUnaffected:\n    \"\"\"Test that integer HSV values are not affected by the bug.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_integer_hue_same_in_both(self):\n        \"\"\"Integer hue values should work the same in buggy and correct.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Integer values should pass through unchanged\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('180', '128', '64')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('180', '128', '64')\n\n        # 1% of 255 = 2.55 → 2 (buggy)\n        # 1% of 359 = 3.59 → 3 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 2\n        assert h_correct == 3\n\n    def test_99_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test: 99% hue should be ~355, not ~252.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv('99%', '99%', '99%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv('99%', '99%', '99%')\n\n        # 99% of 255 = 252.45 → 252 (buggy)\n        # 99% of 359 = 355.41 → 355 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 252\n        assert h_correct == 355\n\n\nclass TestBugSeverity:\n    \"\"\"Tests showing how severe the bug is for various hue values.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize('percent,buggy_expected,correct_expected', [\n        ('0%', 0, 0),           # 0% should always be 0\n        ('10%', 25, 35),        # Off by 10 degrees\n        ('25%', 63, 89),        # Off by 26 degrees\n        ('33%', 84, 118),       # Off by 34 degrees\n        ('50%', 127, 179),      # Off by 52 degrees\n        ('66%', 168, 236),      # Off by 68 degrees\n        ('75%', 191, 269),      # Off by 78 degrees\n        ('90%', 229, 323),      # Off by 94 degrees\n        ('100%', 255, 359),     # Off by 104 degrees\n    ])\n    def test_hue_percentage_scaling_error(self, percent, buggy_expected, correct_expected):\n        \"\"\"Parametrized test showing hue scaling error for various percentages.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv(percent, '50%', '50%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv(percent, '50%', '50%')\n\n        assert h_buggy == buggy_expected, \\\n            f\"Buggy: {percent} hue should be {buggy_expected}, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == correct_expected, \\\n            f\"Correct: {percent} hue should be {correct_expected}, got {h_correct}\"\n\n        # Hue should differ: buggy uses 255, correct uses 359\n        assert h_buggy == 255\n        assert h_correct == 359\n\n        # All other channels should be the same\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 255\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 255\n        assert a_buggy == a_correct == 255\n\n    def test_hsva_bug_50_percent(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: HSVA 50% values with hue scaling difference.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy, a_buggy = buggy.parse_hsva('50%', '50%', '50%', '50%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct, a_correct = correct.parse_hsva('50%', '50%', '50%', '50%')\n\n        # Hue differs: 50% of 255 vs 50% of 359\n        assert h_buggy == 127\n        assert h_correct == 179\n\n        # S, V, A should all be the same (all use 255)\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 127 or s_correct == 128\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 127 or v_correct == 128\n        assert a_buggy == a_correct == 127 or a_correct == 128\n\n\nclass TestIntegerValuesUnaffected:\n    \"\"\"Test that integer HSV values are not affected by the bug.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_integer_hue_same_in_both(self):\n        \"\"\"Integer hue values should work the same in buggy and correct.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Integer values should pass through unchanged\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('180', '128', '64')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('180', '128', '64')\n\n        # Should be identical for integer values\n        assert h_buggy == h_correct == 180\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 128\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 64\n\n    def test_integer_hsva_same_in_both(self):\n        \"\"\"Integer HSVA values should work the same in buggy and correct.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy, a_buggy = buggy.parse_hsva('180', '128', '64', '200')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct, a_correct = correct.parse_hsva('180', '128', '64', '200')\n\n        assert h_buggy == h_correct == 180\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 128\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 64\n        assert a_buggy == a_correct == 200\n\n\nclass TestEdgeCases:\n    \"\"\"Test edge cases in HSV percentage parsing.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_1_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test: 1% hue should be ~3-4, not ~2.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv('1%', '1%', '1%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv('1%', '1%', '1%')\n\n        # This affects both HSV and HSVA color parsing.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport pytest\n\n\nclass MockBuggyHSVParser:\n    \"\"\"Mock implementation of the buggy HSV parser (uses 255 for all channels).\"\"\"\n\n    def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Parse a single HSV/HSVA value - BUGGY VERSION.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return int(val)\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n\n        # BUG: Always use 255 instead of 359 for hue\n        mult = 255.0  # BUGGY - should be 359.0 for hue\n        if val.endswith('%'):\n            val = val[:-1]\n            mult = mult / 100\n\n        try:\n            return int(float(val) * mult)\n        except ValueError:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid color value: {val}\")\n\n    def parse_hsv(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSV color string and return (h, s, v) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)  # h parameter for hue\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)  # s parameter for saturation\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)  # v parameter for value\n        return (h, s, v)\n\n    def parse_hsva(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str, a_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSVA color string and return (h, s, v, a) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)\n        a = self._parse_value('a', a_str)\n        return (h, s, v, a)\n\n\nclass MockCorrectHSVParser:\n    \"\"\"Mock implementation of the correct HSV parser.\"\"\"\n\n    def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Parse a single HSV/HSVA value - CORRECT VERSION.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return int(val)\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n\n        # CORRECT: Use 359 for hue, 255 for other channels\n        mult = 359.0 if kind == 'h' else 255.0\n        if val.endswith('%'):\n            val = val[:-1]\n            mult = mult / 100\n\n        try:\n            return int(float(val) * mult)\n        except ValueError:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid color value: {val}\")\n\n    def parse_hsv(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSV color string and return (h, s, v) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)  # h parameter for hue\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)  # s parameter for saturation\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)  # v parameter for value\n        return (h, s, v)\n\n    def parse_hsva(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str, a_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSVA color string and return (h, s, v, a) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)\n        a = self._parse_value('a', a_str)\n        return (h, s, v, a)\n\n\nclass TestHSVBugDemonstration:\n    \"\"\"Tests that demonstrate the HSV hue scaling bug vs correct behavior.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_bug_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 100% hue should be 359, not 255.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Parse 100% hue with the buggy implementation\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # Parse 100% hue with the correct implementation\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # The bug: hue is scaled to ~255 instead of ~359\n        # Allow for rounding: 100.0 * 2.55 might give 254.99... → 254 or 255\n        assert h_buggy in (254, 255), f\"Buggy parser should give h≈255, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 359, f\"Correct parser should give h=359, got {h_correct}\"\n\n        # Saturation and value should be the same in both\n        assert s_buggy == 255\n        assert s_correct == 255\n        assert v_buggy == 255\n        assert v_correct == 255\n\n    def test_bug_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 50% hue should be ~179, not ~127.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Parse 50% hue\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('50%', '50%', '50%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('50%', '50%', '50%')\n\n        # The bug: 50% of 255 = 127.5 → 127\n        # Correct: 50% of 359 = 179.5 → 179\n        assert h_buggy == 127, f\"Buggy parser should give h=127 for 50%, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 179, f\"Correct parser should give h=179 for 50%, got {h_correct}\"\n\n    def test_bug_25_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 25% hue should be ~89-90, not ~63-64.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv('25%', '25%', '25%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv('25%', '25%', '25%')\n\n        # 25% of 255 = 63.75 → 63 (buggy)\n        # 25% of 359 = 89.75 → 89 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 63, f\"Buggy: 25% hue should be 63, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 89, f\"Correct: 25% hue should be 89, got {h_correct}\"\n\n    def test_bug_10_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 10% hue should be ~35, not ~25.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('10%', '10%', '10%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('10%', '10%', '10%')\n\n        # Hue should differ: buggy uses ~255, correct uses 359\n        assert h_buggy in (254, 255), f\"Buggy HSVA hue should be ~255, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 359\n\n        # All other channels should be the same\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 255\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 255\n        assert a_buggy == a_correct == 255\n\n    def test_hsva_bug_50_percent(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: HSVA 50% values with hue scaling difference.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy, a_buggy = buggy.parse_hsva('50%', '50%', '50%', '50%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct, a_correct = correct.parse_hsva('50%', '50%', '50%', '50%')\n\n        # Hue differs: 50% of 255 vs 50% of 359\n        assert h_buggy == 127\n        assert h_correct == 179\n\n        # S, V, A should all be the same (all use 255)\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 127 or s_correct == 128\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 127 or v_correct == 128\n        assert a_buggy == a_correct == 127 or a_correct == 128\n\n\nclass TestIntegerValuesUnaffected:\n    \"\"\"Test that integer HSV values are not affected by the bug.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_integer_hue_same_in_both(self):\n        \"\"\"Integer hue values should work the same in buggy and correct.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Integer values should pass through unchanged\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('180', '128', '64')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('180', '128', '64')\n\n        # Should be identical for integer values\n        assert h_buggy == h_correct == 180\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 128\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 64\n\n    def test_integer_hsva_same_in_both(self):\n        \"\"\"Integer HSVA values should work the same in buggy and correct.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy, a_buggy = buggy.parse_hsva('180', '128', '64', '200')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct, a_correct = correct.parse_hsva('180', '128', '64', '200')\n\n        assert h_buggy == h_correct == 180\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 128\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 64\n        assert a_buggy == a_correct == 200\n\n\nclass TestEdgeCases:\n    \"\"\"Test edge cases in HSV percentage parsing.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_1_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test: 1% hue should be ~3-4, not ~2.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv('1%', '1%', '1%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv('1%', '1%', '1%')\n\n        # 1% of 255 = 2.55 → 2 (buggy)\n        # 1% of 359 = 3.59 → 3 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 2\n        assert h_correct == 3\n\n    def test_99_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test: 99% hue should be ~355, not ~252.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv('99%', '99%', '99%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv('99%', '99%', '99%')\n\n        # 99% of 255 = 252.45 → 252 (buggy)\n        # 99% of 359 = 355.41 → 355 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 252\n        assert h_correct == 355\n\n\nclass TestBugSeverity:\n    \"\"\"Tests showing how severe the bug is for various hue values.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize('percent,buggy_expected,correct_expected', [\n        ('0%', 0, 0),           # 0% should always be 0\n        ('10%', 25, 35),        # Off by 10 degrees\n        ('25%', 63, 89),        # Off by 26 degrees\n        ('33%', 84, 118),       # Off by 34 degrees\n        ('50%', 127, 179),      # Off by 52 degrees\n        ('66%', 168, 236),      # Off by 68 degrees\n        ('75%', 191, 269),      # Off by 78 degrees\n        ('90%', 229, 323),      # Off by 94 degrees\n        ('100%', 255, 359),     # Off by 104 degrees\n    ])\n    def test_hue_percentage_scaling_error(self, percent, buggy_expected, correct_expected):\n        \"\"\"Parametrized test showing hue scaling error for various percentages.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv(percent, '50%', '50%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv(percent, '50%', '50%')\n\n        assert h_buggy == buggy_expected, \\\n            f\"Buggy: {percent} hue should be {buggy_expected}, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == correct_expected, \\\n            f\"Correct: {percent} hue should be {correct_expected}, got {h_correct}\"\n\n        # Verify they differ\n        assert h_buggy != h_correct, \\\n            f\"Bug not present: buggy and correct should differ for {percent}\"\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    pytest.main([__file__, '-v', '--tb=short'])\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":259,"oldLines":7,"newStart":259,"newLines":6,"lines":["     \"\"\"Tests showing how severe the bug is for various hue values.\"\"\""," ","     @pytest.mark.parametrize('percent,buggy_expected,correct_expected', [","-        ('0%', 0, 0),           # This affects both HSV and HSVA color parsing.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport pytest\n\n\nclass MockBuggyHSVParser:\n    \"\"\"Mock implementation of the buggy HSV parser (uses 255 for all channels).\"\"\"\n\n    def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Parse a single HSV/HSVA value - BUGGY VERSION.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return int(val)\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n\n        # BUG: Always use 255 instead of 359 for hue\n        mult = 255.0  # BUGGY - should be 359.0 for hue\n        if val.endswith('%'):\n            val = val[:-1]\n            mult = mult / 100\n\n        try:\n            return int(float(val) * mult)\n        except ValueError:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid color value: {val}\")\n\n    def parse_hsv(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSV color string and return (h, s, v) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)  # h parameter for hue\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)  # s parameter for saturation\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)  # v parameter for value\n        return (h, s, v)\n\n    def parse_hsva(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str, a_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSVA color string and return (h, s, v, a) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)\n        a = self._parse_value('a', a_str)\n        return (h, s, v, a)\n\n\nclass MockCorrectHSVParser:\n    \"\"\"Mock implementation of the correct HSV parser.\"\"\"\n\n    def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Parse a single HSV/HSVA value - CORRECT VERSION.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return int(val)\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n\n        # CORRECT: Use 359 for hue, 255 for other channels\n        mult = 359.0 if kind == 'h' else 255.0\n        if val.endswith('%'):\n            val = val[:-1]\n            mult = mult / 100\n\n        try:\n            return int(float(val) * mult)\n        except ValueError:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid color value: {val}\")\n\n    def parse_hsv(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSV color string and return (h, s, v) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)  # h parameter for hue\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)  # s parameter for saturation\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)  # v parameter for value\n        return (h, s, v)\n\n    def parse_hsva(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str, a_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSVA color string and return (h, s, v, a) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)\n        a = self._parse_value('a', a_str)\n        return (h, s, v, a)\n\n\nclass TestHSVBugDemonstration:\n    \"\"\"Tests that demonstrate the HSV hue scaling bug vs correct behavior.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_bug_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 100% hue should be 359, not 255.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Parse 100% hue with the buggy implementation\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # Parse 100% hue with the correct implementation\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # The bug: hue is scaled to ~255 instead of ~359\n        # Allow for rounding: 100.0 * 2.55 might give 254.99... → 254 or 255\n        assert h_buggy in (254, 255), f\"Buggy parser should give h≈255, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 359, f\"Correct parser should give h=359, got {h_correct}\"\n\n        # Saturation and value should be the same in both\n        assert s_buggy == 255\n        assert s_correct == 255\n        assert v_buggy == 255\n        assert v_correct == 255\n\n    def test_bug_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 50% hue should be ~179, not ~127.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Parse 50% hue\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('50%', '50%', '50%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('50%', '50%', '50%')\n\n        # The bug: 50% of 255 = 127.5 → 127\n        # Correct: 50% of 359 = 179.5 → 179\n        assert h_buggy == 127, f\"Buggy parser should give h=127 for 50%, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 179, f\"Correct parser should give h=179 for 50%, got {h_correct}\"\n\n    def test_bug_25_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 25% hue should be ~89-90, not ~63-64.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv('25%', '25%', '25%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv('25%', '25%', '25%')\n\n        # 25% of 255 = 63.75 → 63 (buggy)\n        # 25% of 359 = 89.75 → 89 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 63, f\"Buggy: 25% hue should be 63, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 89, f\"Correct: 25% hue should be 89, got {h_correct}\"\n\n    def test_bug_10_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 10% hue should be ~35, not ~25.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('10%', '10%', '10%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('10%', '10%', '10%')\n\n        # 10% of 255 = 25.5 → 25 (buggy)\n        # 10% of 359 = 35.9 → 35 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 25, f\"Buggy: 10% hue should be 25, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 35, f\"Correct: 10% hue should be 35, got {h_correct}\"\n\n        # Hue differs: 50% of 255 vs 50% of 359\n        assert h_buggy == 127\n        assert h_correct == 179\n\n        # S, V, A should all be the same (all use 255)\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 127 or s_correct == 128\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 127 or v_correct == 128\n        assert a_buggy == a_correct == 127 or a_correct == 128\n\n\nclass TestIntegerValuesUnaffected:\n    \"\"\"Test that integer HSV values are not affected by the bug.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_integer_hue_same_in_both(self):\n        \"\"\"Integer hue values should work the same in buggy and correct.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Off by 68 degrees\n        ('75%', 191, 269),      # Off by 78 degrees\n        ('90%', 229, 323),      # Off by 94 degrees\n        ('100%', (254, 255), 359),     # Off by 104 degrees (allow rounding)\n    ])\n    def test_hue_percentage_scaling_error(self, percent, buggy_expected, correct_expected):\n        \"\"\"Parametrized test showing hue scaling error for various percentages.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv(percent, '50%', '50%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv(percent, '50%', '50%')\n\n        # 25% of 255 = 63.75 → 63 (buggy)\n        # 25% of 359 = 89.75 → 89 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 63, f\"Buggy: 25% hue should be 63, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 89, f\"Correct: 25% hue should be 89, got {h_correct}\"\n\n    def test_bug_10_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 10% hue should be ~35, not ~25.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('10%', '10%', '10%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('10%', '10%', '10%')\n\n        # Both should have same saturation/value since both use 255\n        assert s_buggy == 25 or s_buggy == 26  # int(10% * 255) = int(25.5)\n        assert v_buggy == 25 or v_buggy == 26\n        assert s_correct == 25 or s_correct == 26\n        assert v_correct == 25 or v_correct == 26\n\n\nclass TestHSVABugDemonstration:\n    \"\"\"Tests that demonstrate the HSVA (with alpha) hue scaling bug.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_hsva_bug_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: HSVA 100% hue should be 359, not 255.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy, a_buggy = buggy.parse_hsva('100%', '100%', '100%', '100%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct, a_correct = correct.parse_hsva('100%', '100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # Hue should differ: buggy uses ~255, correct uses 359\n        assert h_buggy in (254, 255), f\"Buggy HSVA hue should be ~255, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 359\n\n        # All other channels should be the same\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 255\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 255\n        assert a_buggy == a_correct == 255\n\n    def test_hsva_bug_50_percent(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: HSVA 50% values with hue scaling difference.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy, a_buggy = buggy.parse_hsva('50%', '50%', '50%', '50%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct, a_correct = correct.parse_hsva('50%', '50%', '50%', '50%')\n\n        # Hue differs: 50% of 255 vs 50% of 359\n        assert h_buggy == 127\n        assert h_correct == 179\n\n        # S, V, A should all be the same (all use 255)\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 127 or s_correct == 128\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 127 or v_correct == 128\n        assert a_buggy == a_correct == 127 or a_correct == 128\n\n\nclass TestIntegerValuesUnaffected:\n    \"\"\"Test that integer HSV values are not affected by the bug.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_integer_hue_same_in_both(self):\n        \"\"\"Integer hue values should work the same in buggy and correct.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Integer values should pass through unchanged\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('180', '128', '64')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('180', '128', '64')\n\n        # Should be identical for integer values\n        assert h_buggy == h_correct == 180\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 128\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 64\n\n    def test_integer_hsva_same_in_both(self):\n        \"\"\"Integer HSVA values should work the same in buggy and correct.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy, a_buggy = buggy.parse_hsva('180', '128', '64', '200')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct, a_correct = correct.parse_hsva('180', '128', '64', '200')\n\n        assert h_buggy == h_correct == 180\n        assert s_buggy == s_correct == 128\n        assert v_buggy == v_correct == 64\n        assert a_buggy == a_correct == 200\n\n\nclass TestEdgeCases:\n    \"\"\"Test edge cases in HSV percentage parsing.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_1_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test: 1% hue should be ~3-4, not ~2.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv('1%', '1%', '1%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv('1%', '1%', '1%')\n\n        # 1% of 255 = 2.55 → 2 (buggy)\n        # 1% of 359 = 3.59 → 3 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 2\n        assert h_correct == 3\n\n    def test_99_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Test: 99% hue should be ~355, not ~252.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv('99%', '99%', '99%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv('99%', '99%', '99%')\n\n        # 99% of 255 = 252.45 → 252 (buggy)\n        # 99% of 359 = 355.41 → 355 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 252\n        assert h_correct == 355\n\n\nclass TestBugSeverity:\n    \"\"\"Tests showing how severe the bug is for various hue values.\"\"\"\n\n    @pytest.mark.parametrize('percent,buggy_expected,correct_expected', [\n        ('10%', 25, 35),        # Off by 10 degrees\n        ('25%', 63, 89),        # Off by 26 degrees\n        ('33%', 84, 118),       # Off by 34 degrees\n        ('50%', 127, 179),      # Off by 52 degrees\n        ('66%', 168, 236),      # Off by 68 degrees\n        ('75%', 191, 269),      # Off by 78 degrees\n        ('90%', 229, 323),      # Off by 94 degrees\n        ('100%', (254, 255), 359),     # Off by 104 degrees (allow rounding)\n    ])\n    def test_hue_percentage_scaling_error(self, percent, buggy_expected, correct_expected):\n        \"\"\"Parametrized test showing hue scaling error for various percentages.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv(percent, '50%', '50%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv(percent, '50%', '50%')\n\n        # Handle tuple for expected values (for rounding tolerance)\n        if isinstance(buggy_expected, tuple):\n            assert h_buggy in buggy_expected, \\\n                f\"Buggy: {percent} hue should be in {buggy_expected}, got {h_buggy}\"\n        else:\n            assert h_buggy == buggy_expected, \\\n                f\"Buggy: {percent} hue should be {buggy_expected}, got {h_buggy}\"\n\n        assert h_correct == correct_expected, \\\n            f\"Correct: {percent} hue should be {correct_expected}, got {h_correct}\"\n\n        # Verify they differ (except for special cases where values might be equal)\n        if not (isinstance(buggy_expected, tuple) and h_correct in buggy_expected):\n            assert h_buggy != h_correct, \\\n                f\"Bug not present: buggy and correct should differ for {percent}\"\n\n\nif __name__ == '__main__':\n    pytest.main([__file__, '-v', '--tb=short'])\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":171,"oldLines":10,"newStart":171,"newLines":13,"lines":["         assert h_buggy in (254, 255), f\"Buggy HSVA hue should be ~255, got {h_buggy}\"","         assert h_correct == 359"," ","-        # This affects both HSV and HSVA color parsing.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport pytest\n\n\nclass MockBuggyHSVParser:\n    \"\"\"Mock implementation of the buggy HSV parser (uses 255 for all channels).\"\"\"\n\n    def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Parse a single HSV/HSVA value - BUGGY VERSION.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return int(val)\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n\n        # BUG: Always use 255 instead of 359 for hue\n        mult = 255.0  # BUGGY - should be 359.0 for hue\n        if val.endswith('%'):\n            val = val[:-1]\n            mult = mult / 100\n\n        try:\n            return int(float(val) * mult)\n        except ValueError:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid color value: {val}\")\n\n    def parse_hsv(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSV color string and return (h, s, v) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)  # h parameter for hue\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)  # s parameter for saturation\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)  # v parameter for value\n        return (h, s, v)\n\n    def parse_hsva(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str, a_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSVA color string and return (h, s, v, a) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)\n        a = self._parse_value('a', a_str)\n        return (h, s, v, a)\n\n\nclass MockCorrectHSVParser:\n    \"\"\"Mock implementation of the correct HSV parser.\"\"\"\n\n    def _parse_value(self, kind: str, val: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Parse a single HSV/HSVA value - CORRECT VERSION.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            return int(val)\n        except ValueError:\n            pass\n\n        # CORRECT: Use 359 for hue, 255 for other channels\n        mult = 359.0 if kind == 'h' else 255.0\n        if val.endswith('%'):\n            val = val[:-1]\n            mult = mult / 100\n\n        try:\n            return int(float(val) * mult)\n        except ValueError:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Invalid color value: {val}\")\n\n    def parse_hsv(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSV color string and return (h, s, v) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)  # h parameter for hue\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)  # s parameter for saturation\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)  # v parameter for value\n        return (h, s, v)\n\n    def parse_hsva(self, h_str: str, s_str: str, v_str: str, a_str: str) -> tuple:\n        \"\"\"Parse HSVA color string and return (h, s, v, a) tuple.\"\"\"\n        h = self._parse_value('h', h_str)\n        s = self._parse_value('s', s_str)\n        v = self._parse_value('v', v_str)\n        a = self._parse_value('a', a_str)\n        return (h, s, v, a)\n\n\nclass TestHSVBugDemonstration:\n    \"\"\"Tests that demonstrate the HSV hue scaling bug vs correct behavior.\"\"\"\n\n    def test_bug_100_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 100% hue should be 359, not 255.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Parse 100% hue with the buggy implementation\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # Parse 100% hue with the correct implementation\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('100%', '100%', '100%')\n\n        # The bug: hue is scaled to ~255 instead of ~359\n        # Allow for rounding: 100.0 * 2.55 might give 254.99... → 254 or 255\n        assert h_buggy in (254, 255), f\"Buggy parser should give h≈255, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 359, f\"Correct parser should give h=359, got {h_correct}\"\n\n        # Saturation and value should be the same in both (allow for rounding)\n        assert s_buggy in (254, 255), f\"Saturation buggy should be ~255, got {s_buggy}\"\n        assert s_correct == 255\n        assert v_buggy in (254, 255), f\"Value buggy should be ~255, got {v_buggy}\"\n        assert v_correct == 255\n\n    def test_bug_50_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 50% hue should be ~179, not ~127.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        # Parse 50% hue\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('50%', '50%', '50%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('50%', '50%', '50%')\n\n        # The bug: 50% of 255 = 127.5 → 127\n        # Correct: 50% of 359 = 179.5 → 179\n        assert h_buggy == 127, f\"Buggy parser should give h=127 for 50%, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 179, f\"Correct parser should give h=179 for 50%, got {h_correct}\"\n\n    def test_bug_25_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 25% hue should be ~89-90, not ~63-64.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, _, _ = buggy.parse_hsv('25%', '25%', '25%')\n        h_correct, _, _ = correct.parse_hsv('25%', '25%', '25%')\n\n        # 25% of 255 = 63.75 → 63 (buggy)\n        # 25% of 359 = 89.75 → 89 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 63, f\"Buggy: 25% hue should be 63, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 89, f\"Correct: 25% hue should be 89, got {h_correct}\"\n\n    def test_bug_10_percent_hue(self):\n        \"\"\"Demonstrate: 10% hue should be ~35, not ~25.\"\"\"\n        buggy = MockBuggyHSVParser()\n        correct = MockCorrectHSVParser()\n\n        h_buggy, s_buggy, v_buggy = buggy.parse_hsv('10%', '10%', '10%')\n        h_correct, s_correct, v_correct = correct.parse_hsv('10%', '10%', '10%')\n\n        # 10% of 255 = 25.5 → 25 (buggy)\n        # 10% of 359 = 35.9 → 35 (correct)\n        assert h_buggy == 25, f\"Buggy: 10% hue should be 25, got {h_buggy}\"\n        assert h_correct == 35, f\"Correct: 10% hue should be 35, got {h_correct}\"\n\n        #
